revision.text:
[[Category:皇大神社 (福知山市)]][[Category:New pages]][[ja:皇大神社 (福知山市)]] {{Shrine |name = Kōtai Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kotaijinzya shrine.jpg|280px]]
Entrance |location = {{ill|Fukuchiyama|ja|福知山市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}} Ōe-chō Naikū Aza Miyama 217 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|25|48.5|N|135|09|16.2|E|region:JP-26|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社}} |Established = Traditionally, 39th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} |Main hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} (thatch roof) |Other names = Moto-Ise Kōtai Jingū, Moto-Ise Naikū |Festival date = {{ill|April 26|en|4月26日}} |Rituals = Hassaku Parade ({{ill|September 1|en|9月1日}}) }} '''Kōtai Shrine''' (皇大神社, '''Kōtai Jinja''') is a shrine located in the Ōe district of {{ill|Fukuchiyama|ja|福知山市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Moto-Ise|ja|元伊勢}} legend as one of the temporary enshrinements of {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} before settling in {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. == Overview == Amaterasu Ōmikami, now enshrined at {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, was traditionally worshipped within the Imperial Palace. To relieve the awe of this presence, Emperor Sujin ordered the search for a suitable enshrinement location in the 6th year of his reign. After many relocations, the deity settled in Ise in the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign. The temporary enshrinement sites along this journey are remembered as the {{ill|Moto-Ise|ja|元伊勢}}. Kōtai Shrine is one of these Moto-Ise sites, located at the historical location known as Yoshisa-no-Miya (吉佐宮). As such, it is also referred to as {{ill|Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine|ja|元伊勢内宮 皇大神社}}, reflecting its connection to the {{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|皇大神宮}} of Ise Grand Shrine. Other Moto-Ise shrines in the Ōe area are: {|class="wikitable" |- !colspan="3" | Moto-Ise Shrines of Fukuchiyama City |- |'''Kōtai Shrine''' |Ōe-chō Naikū |Associated with the Inner Shrine (Naikū) of Ise Grand Shrine |- |'''{{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社}}''' |Ōe-chō Tadauchi |Associated with the Outer Shrine (Gekū) of Ise Grand Shrine |- |'''{{ill|Ama-no-Iwato Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|天岩戸神社 (福知山市)}}''' |Ōe-chō Busshōji |Kōtai Shrine’s auxiliary shrine |} Additional shrines also claim Yoshisa-no-Miya heritage, including {{ill|Kono Shrine|ja|籠神社#境外社}}, {{ill|Sasahara Shrine|ja|笶原神社}}, and {{ill|Takeno Shrine|ja|竹野神社}}. == Deity == * '''{{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}}''' - Ancestral goddess of the Imperial family and deity of Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine). == History == According to the ''{{ill|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記}}'', in the 39th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|豊鍬入姫命}} brought Amaterasu Ōmikami to Yoshisa-no-Miya in Tanba Province for four years. Kōtai Shrine is believed to stand on this site, commemorating this temporary enshrinement. The shrine's records state that it was later reconstructed during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}} (707–715). Alternative traditions link the shrine to {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子}}, who reportedly established it during his suppression of local rebels. By the Edo period, Kōtai Shrine had significant support, including land grants and periodic reconstructions by the lords of Miyazu Domain. == Grounds == File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 本殿.JPG|'''Main Hall'''
Shinmei-zukuri with thatched roof. Features inward-curving {{ill|Chigi (architecture)|ja|千木}} and {{ill|Katsuogi|ja|鰹木}}. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 拝殿.JPG|'''Worship Hall''' File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 入口.JPG|'''Torii'''
A blackwood torii, similar to those at {{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社}} and {{ill|Nonomiya Shrine|ja|野宮神社}}. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 和泉式部歌塚.JPG|'''Izumi Shikibu Monument''' The main hall is surrounded by 83 auxiliary shrines arranged in a U-shape. * Sacred Cedar of Ryūtō * Cedar planted by {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子}} == Subsidiary Shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === Located to the left and right of the main hall: * Takuha Chichihime Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Takuha Chichihime|ja|栲幡千千姫命}} * Amano-Tajikarao Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Amano-Tajikarao|ja|天手力雄命}} === Other Shrines === * 83 shrines surrounding the main hall. == Festivals == * '''Hassaku Festival''' (September 1): Includes a procession inspired by the feudal lord’s pilgrimage, reenacted with a daimyo-style parade. == Access == ; Address * Ōe-chō Naikū, Fukuchiyama City, Kyoto Prefecture 217 ; Transportation * '''By Train:''' 15-minute walk from {{ill|Ōeyamaguchi-Naikū Station|ja|大江山口内宮駅}} on the {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道}}. * '''By Car:''' Accessible via Kyoto Prefecture Route 9. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{commons cat|Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)}} * [http://motoise-naiku.com/ Official Website of Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine] * [https://www.uminokyoto.jp/genryu/fukuchiyama/page4.html Moto-Ise Shrines - Fukuchiyama City - Sea of Kyoto] (Kyoto Northern Region Promotion Association) {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fukuchiyama]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Fukuchiyama]] [[Category:Former Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Moto-Ise]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Miyazu Domain]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:New pages]][[Category:New pages]][[ja:皇大神社 (福知山市)]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[ja:皇大神社]]

Shinmei-zukuri with thatched roof. Features inward-curving {{ill|Chigi (architecture)|ja|千木}} and {{ill|Katsuogi|ja|鰹木}}. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 拝殿.JPG|'''Worship Hall''' File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 入口.JPG|'''Torii'''

File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 和泉式部歌塚.JPG|'''Izumi Shikibu Monument''' The main hall is surrounded by 83 auxiliary shrines arranged in a U-shape. * Sacred Cedar of Ryūtō * Cedar planted by {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子}} == Subsidiary Shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === Located to the left and right of the main hall: * Takuha Chichihime Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Takuha Chichihime|ja|栲幡千千姫命}} * Amano-Tajikarao Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Amano-Tajikarao|ja|天手力雄命}} === Other Shrines === * 83 shrines surrounding the main hall. == Festivals == * '''Hassaku Festival''' (September 1): Includes a procession inspired by the feudal lord’s pilgrimage, reenacted with a daimyo-style parade. == Access == ; Address * Ōe-chō Naikū, Fukuchiyama City, Kyoto Prefecture 217 ; Transportation * '''By Train:''' 15-minute walk from {{ill|Ōeyamaguchi-Naikū Station|ja|大江山口内宮駅}} on the {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道}}. * '''By Car:''' Accessible via Kyoto Prefecture Route 9. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{commons cat|Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)}} * [http://motoise-naiku.com/ Official Website of Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine] * [https://www.uminokyoto.jp/genryu/fukuchiyama/page4.html Moto-Ise Shrines - Fukuchiyama City - Sea of Kyoto] (Kyoto Northern Region Promotion Association) {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fukuchiyama]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Fukuchiyama]] [[Category:Former Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Moto-Ise]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Miyazu Domain]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:New pages]][[Category:New pages]][[ja:皇大神社 (福知山市)]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[ja:皇大神社]]

[[Category:皇大神社 (福知山市)]][[Category:New pages]][[ja:皇大神社 (福知山市)]] {{Shrine |name = Kōtai Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kotaijinzya shrine.jpg|280px]]
Entrance |location = {{ill|Fukuchiyama|ja|福知山市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}} Ōe-chō Naikū Aza Miyama 217 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|25|48.5|N|135|09|16.2|E|region:JP-26|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社}} |Established = Traditionally, 39th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} |Main hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} (thatch roof) |Other names = Moto-Ise Kōtai Jingū, Moto-Ise Naikū |Festival date = {{ill|April 26|en|4月26日}} |Rituals = Hassaku Parade ({{ill|September 1|en|9月1日}}) }} '''Kōtai Shrine''' (皇大神社, '''Kōtai Jinja''') is a shrine located in the Ōe district of {{ill|Fukuchiyama|ja|福知山市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Moto-Ise|ja|元伊勢}} legend as one of the temporary enshrinements of {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} before settling in {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. == Overview == Amaterasu Ōmikami, now enshrined at {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, was traditionally worshipped within the Imperial Palace. To relieve the awe of this presence, Emperor Sujin ordered the search for a suitable enshrinement location in the 6th year of his reign. After many relocations, the deity settled in Ise in the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign. The temporary enshrinement sites along this journey are remembered as the {{ill|Moto-Ise|ja|元伊勢}}. Kōtai Shrine is one of these Moto-Ise sites, located at the historical location known as Yoshisa-no-Miya (吉佐宮). As such, it is also referred to as {{ill|Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine|ja|元伊勢内宮 皇大神社}}, reflecting its connection to the {{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|皇大神宮}} of Ise Grand Shrine. Other Moto-Ise shrines in the Ōe area are: {|class="wikitable" |- !colspan="3" | Moto-Ise Shrines of Fukuchiyama City |- |'''Kōtai Shrine''' |Ōe-chō Naikū |Associated with the Inner Shrine (Naikū) of Ise Grand Shrine |- |'''{{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社}}''' |Ōe-chō Tadauchi |Associated with the Outer Shrine (Gekū) of Ise Grand Shrine |- |'''{{ill|Ama-no-Iwato Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|天岩戸神社 (福知山市)}}''' |Ōe-chō Busshōji |Kōtai Shrine’s auxiliary shrine |} Additional shrines also claim Yoshisa-no-Miya heritage, including {{ill|Kono Shrine|ja|籠神社#境外社}}, {{ill|Sasahara Shrine|ja|笶原神社}}, and {{ill|Takeno Shrine|ja|竹野神社}}. == Deity == * '''{{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}}''' - Ancestral goddess of the Imperial family and deity of Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine). == History == According to the ''{{ill|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記}}'', in the 39th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|豊鍬入姫命}} brought Amaterasu Ōmikami to Yoshisa-no-Miya in Tanba Province for four years. Kōtai Shrine is believed to stand on this site, commemorating this temporary enshrinement. The shrine's records state that it was later reconstructed during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}} (707–715). Alternative traditions link the shrine to {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子}}, who reportedly established it during his suppression of local rebels. By the Edo period, Kōtai Shrine had significant support, including land grants and periodic reconstructions by the lords of Miyazu Domain. == Grounds == File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 本殿.JPG|'''Main Hall'''
Shinmei-zukuri with thatched roof. Features inward-curving {{ill|Chigi (architecture)|ja|千木}} and {{ill|Katsuogi|ja|鰹木}}. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 拝殿.JPG|'''Worship Hall''' File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 入口.JPG|'''Torii'''
A blackwood torii, similar to those at {{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社}} and {{ill|Nonomiya Shrine|ja|野宮神社}}. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 和泉式部歌塚.JPG|'''Izumi Shikibu Monument''' The main hall is surrounded by 83 auxiliary shrines arranged in a U-shape. * Sacred Cedar of Ryūtō * Cedar planted by {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子}} == Subsidiary Shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === Located to the left and right of the main hall: * Takuha Chichihime Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Takuha Chichihime|ja|栲幡千千姫命}} * Amano-Tajikarao Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Amano-Tajikarao|ja|天手力雄命}} === Other Shrines === * 83 shrines surrounding the main hall. == Festivals == * '''Hassaku Festival''' (September 1): Includes a procession inspired by the feudal lord’s pilgrimage, reenacted with a daimyo-style parade. == Access == ; Address * Ōe-chō Naikū, Fukuchiyama City, Kyoto Prefecture 217 ; Transportation * '''By Train:''' 15-minute walk from {{ill|Ōeyamaguchi-Naikū Station|ja|大江山口内宮駅}} on the {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道}}. * '''By Car:''' Accessible via Kyoto Prefecture Route 9. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{commons cat|Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)}} * [http://motoise-naiku.com/ Official Website of Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine] * [https://www.uminokyoto.jp/genryu/fukuchiyama/page4.html Moto-Ise Shrines - Fukuchiyama City - Sea of Kyoto] (Kyoto Northern Region Promotion Association) {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fukuchiyama]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Fukuchiyama]] [[Category:Former Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Moto-Ise]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Miyazu Domain]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:New pages]][[Category:New pages]][[ja:皇大神社 (福知山市)]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[ja:皇大神社]]

It is part of the {{ill|Moto-Ise|ja|元伊勢}} legend as one of the temporary enshrinements of {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} before settling in {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. == Overview == Amaterasu Ōmikami, now enshrined at {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, was traditionally worshipped within the Imperial Palace. To relieve the awe of this presence, Emperor Sujin ordered the search for a suitable enshrinement location in the 6th year of his reign. After many relocations, the deity settled in Ise in the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign. The temporary enshrinement sites along this journey are remembered as the {{ill|Moto-Ise|ja|元伊勢}}. Kōtai Shrine is one of these Moto-Ise sites, located at the historical location known as Yoshisa-no-Miya (吉佐宮). As such, it is also referred to as {{ill|Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine|ja|元伊勢内宮 皇大神社}}, reflecting its connection to the {{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|皇大神宮}} of Ise Grand Shrine. Other Moto-Ise shrines in the Ōe area are: {|class="wikitable" |- !colspan="3" | Moto-Ise Shrines of Fukuchiyama City |- |'''Kōtai Shrine''' |Ōe-chō Naikū |Associated with the Inner Shrine (Naikū) of Ise Grand Shrine |- |'''{{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社}}''' |Ōe-chō Tadauchi |Associated with the Outer Shrine (Gekū) of Ise Grand Shrine |- |'''{{ill|Ama-no-Iwato Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|天岩戸神社 (福知山市)}}''' |Ōe-chō Busshōji |Kōtai Shrine’s auxiliary shrine |} Additional shrines also claim Yoshisa-no-Miya heritage, including {{ill|Kono Shrine|ja|籠神社#境外社}}, {{ill|Sasahara Shrine|ja|笶原神社}}, and {{ill|Takeno Shrine|ja|竹野神社}}. == Deity == * '''{{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}}''' - Ancestral goddess of the Imperial family and deity of Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine). == History == According to the ''{{ill|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記}}'', in the 39th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|豊鍬入姫命}} brought Amaterasu Ōmikami to Yoshisa-no-Miya in Tanba Province for four years. Kōtai Shrine is believed to stand on this site, commemorating this temporary enshrinement. The shrine's records state that it was later reconstructed during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}} (707–715). Alternative traditions link the shrine to {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子}}, who reportedly established it during his suppression of local rebels. By the Edo period, Kōtai Shrine had significant support, including land grants and periodic reconstructions by the lords of Miyazu Domain. == Grounds == File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 本殿.JPG|'''Main Hall'''
Shinmei-zukuri with thatched roof. Features inward-curving {{ill|Chigi (architecture)|ja|千木}} and {{ill|Katsuogi|ja|鰹木}}. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 拝殿.JPG|'''Worship Hall''' File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 入口.JPG|'''Torii'''
A blackwood torii, similar to those at {{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社}} and {{ill|Nonomiya Shrine|ja|野宮神社}}. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 和泉式部歌塚.JPG|'''Izumi Shikibu Monument''' The main hall is surrounded by 83 auxiliary shrines arranged in a U-shape. * Sacred Cedar of Ryūtō * Cedar planted by {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子}} == Subsidiary Shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === Located to the left and right of the main hall: * Takuha Chichihime Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Takuha Chichihime|ja|栲幡千千姫命}} * Amano-Tajikarao Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Amano-Tajikarao|ja|天手力雄命}} === Other Shrines === * 83 shrines surrounding the main hall. == Festivals == * '''Hassaku Festival''' (September 1): Includes a procession inspired by the feudal lord’s pilgrimage, reenacted with a daimyo-style parade. == Access == ; Address * Ōe-chō Naikū, Fukuchiyama City, Kyoto Prefecture 217 ; Transportation * '''By Train:''' 15-minute walk from {{ill|Ōeyamaguchi-Naikū Station|ja|大江山口内宮駅}} on the {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道}}. * '''By Car:''' Accessible via Kyoto Prefecture Route 9. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{commons cat|Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)}} * [http://motoise-naiku.com/ Official Website of Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine] * [https://www.uminokyoto.jp/genryu/fukuchiyama/page4.html Moto-Ise Shrines - Fukuchiyama City - Sea of Kyoto] (Kyoto Northern Region Promotion Association) {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fukuchiyama]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Fukuchiyama]] [[Category:Former Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Moto-Ise]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Miyazu Domain]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[ja:皇大神社 (福知山市)]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[ja:皇大神社]]

[[Category:皇大神社 (福知山市)]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]][[ja:皇大神社 (福知山市)]] {{Shrine |name = Kōtai Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kotaijinzya shrine.jpg|280px]]
Entrance |location = {{ill|Fukuchiyama|ja|福知山市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}} Ōe-chō Naikū Aza Miyama 217 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|25|48.5|N|135|09|16.2|E|region:JP-26|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社}} |Established = Traditionally, 39th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} |Main hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} (thatch roof) |Other names = Moto-Ise Kōtai Jingū, Moto-Ise Naikū |Festival date = {{ill|April 26|en|4月26日}} |Rituals = Hassaku Parade ({{ill|September 1|en|9月1日}}) }} '''Kōtai Shrine''' (皇大神社, '''Kōtai Jinja''') is a shrine located in the Ōe district of {{ill|Fukuchiyama|ja|福知山市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Moto-Ise|ja|元伊勢}} legend as one of the temporary enshrinements of {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} before settling in {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. == Overview == Amaterasu Ōmikami, now enshrined at {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, was traditionally worshipped within the Imperial Palace. To relieve the awe of this presence, Emperor Sujin ordered the search for a suitable enshrinement location in the 6th year of his reign. After many relocations, the deity settled in Ise in the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign. The temporary enshrinement sites along this journey are remembered as the {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢}}. Kōtai Shrine is one of these Moto-Ise sites, located at the historical location known as Yoshisa-no-Miya (吉佐宮). As such, it is also referred to as {{ill|Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine|ja|元伊勢内宮 皇大神社}}, reflecting its connection to the {{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|皇大神宮}} of Ise Grand Shrine. Other Moto-Ise shrines in the Ōe area are: {|class="wikitable" |- !colspan="3" | Moto-Ise Shrines of Fukuchiyama City |- |'''Kōtai Shrine''' |Ōe-chō Naikū |Associated with the Inner Shrine (Naikū) of Ise Grand Shrine |- |'''{{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社}}''' |Ōe-chō Tadauchi |Associated with the Outer Shrine (Gekū) of Ise Grand Shrine |- |'''{{ill|Ama-no-Iwato Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|天岩戸神社 (福知山市)}}''' |Ōe-chō Busshōji |Kōtai Shrine’s auxiliary shrine |} Additional shrines also claim Yoshisa-no-Miya heritage, including {{ill|Kono Shrine|ja|籠神社#境外社}}, {{ill|Sasahara Shrine|ja|笶原神社}}, and {{ill|Takeno Shrine|ja|竹野神社}}. == Deity == * '''{{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}}''' - Ancestral goddess of the Imperial family and deity of Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine). == History == According to the ''{{ill|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記}}'', in the 39th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|豊鍬入姫命}} brought Amaterasu Ōmikami to Yoshisa-no-Miya in Tanba Province for four years. Kōtai Shrine is believed to stand on this site, commemorating this temporary enshrinement. The shrine's records state that it was later reconstructed during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}} (707–715). Alternative traditions link the shrine to {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子}}, who reportedly established it during his suppression of local rebels. By the Edo period, Kōtai Shrine had significant support, including land grants and periodic reconstructions by the lords of Miyazu Domain. == Grounds == File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 本殿.JPG|'''Main Hall'''
Shinmei-zukuri with thatched roof. Features inward-curving {{ill|Chigi (architecture)|ja|千木}} and {{ill|Katsuogi|ja|鰹木}}. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 拝殿.JPG|'''Worship Hall''' File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 入口.JPG|'''Torii'''
A blackwood torii, similar to those at {{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社}} and {{ill|Nonomiya Shrine|ja|野宮神社}}. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 和泉式部歌塚.JPG|'''Izumi Shikibu Monument''' The main hall is surrounded by 83 auxiliary shrines arranged in a U-shape. * Sacred Cedar of Ryūtō * Cedar planted by {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子}} == Subsidiary Shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === Located to the left and right of the main hall: * Takuha Chichihime Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Takuha Chichihime|ja|栲幡千千姫命}} * Amano-Tajikarao Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Amano-Tajikarao|ja|天手力雄命}} === Other Shrines === * 83 shrines surrounding the main hall. == Festivals == * '''Hassaku Festival''' (September 1): Includes a procession inspired by the feudal lord’s pilgrimage, reenacted with a daimyo-style parade. == Access == ; Address * Ōe-chō Naikū, Fukuchiyama City, Kyoto Prefecture 217 ; Transportation * '''By Train:''' 15-minute walk from {{ill|Ōeyamaguchi-Naikū Station|ja|大江山口内宮駅}} on the {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道}}. * '''By Car:''' Accessible via Kyoto Prefecture Route 9. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{commons cat|Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)}} * [http://motoise-naiku.com/ Official Website of Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine] * [https://www.uminokyoto.jp/genryu/fukuchiyama/page4.html Moto-Ise Shrines - Fukuchiyama City - Sea of Kyoto] (Kyoto Northern Region Promotion Association) {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fukuchiyama]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Fukuchiyama]] [[Category:Former Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Moto-Ise]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Miyazu Domain]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]][[ja:皇大神社 (福知山市)]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:nnn]] [[ja:皇大神社]]

Entrance |location = {{ill|Fukuchiyama|ja|福知山市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}} Ōe-chō Naikū Aza Miyama 217 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|25|48.5|N|135|09|16.2|E|region:JP-26|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社}} |Established = Traditionally, 39th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} |Main hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} (thatch roof) |Other names = Moto-Ise Kōtai Jingū, Moto-Ise Naikū |Festival date = {{ill|April 26|en|4月26日}} |Rituals = Hassaku Parade ({{ill|September 1|en|9月1日}}) }} '''Kōtai Shrine''' (皇大神社, '''Kōtai Jinja''') is a shrine located in the Ōe district of {{ill|Fukuchiyama|ja|福知山市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Moto-Ise|ja|元伊勢}} legend as one of the temporary enshrinements of {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} before settling in {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. == Overview == Amaterasu Ōmikami, now enshrined at {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, was traditionally worshipped within the Imperial Palace. To relieve the awe of this presence, Emperor Sujin ordered the search for a suitable enshrinement location in the 6th year of his reign. After many relocations, the deity settled in Ise in the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign. The temporary enshrinement sites along this journey are remembered as the {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢}}. Kōtai Shrine is one of these Moto-Ise sites, located at the historical location known as Yoshisa-no-Miya (吉佐宮). As such, it is also referred to as {{ill|Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine|ja|元伊勢内宮 皇大神社}}, reflecting its connection to the {{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|皇大神宮}} of Ise Grand Shrine. Other Moto-Ise shrines in the Ōe area are: {|class="wikitable" |- !colspan="3" | Moto-Ise Shrines of Fukuchiyama City |- |'''Kōtai Shrine''' |Ōe-chō Naikū |Associated with the Inner Shrine (Naikū) of Ise Grand Shrine |- |'''{{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社}}''' |Ōe-chō Tadauchi |Associated with the Outer Shrine (Gekū) of Ise Grand Shrine |- |'''{{ill|Ama-no-Iwato Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|天岩戸神社 (福知山市)}}''' |Ōe-chō Busshōji |Kōtai Shrine’s auxiliary shrine |} Additional shrines also claim Yoshisa-no-Miya heritage, including {{ill|Kono Shrine|ja|籠神社#境外社}}, {{ill|Sasahara Shrine|ja|笶原神社}}, and {{ill|Takeno Shrine|ja|竹野神社}}. == Deity == * '''{{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}}''' - Ancestral goddess of the Imperial family and deity of Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine). == History == According to the ''{{ill|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記}}'', in the 39th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|豊鍬入姫命}} brought Amaterasu Ōmikami to Yoshisa-no-Miya in Tanba Province for four years. Kōtai Shrine is believed to stand on this site, commemorating this temporary enshrinement. The shrine's records state that it was later reconstructed during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}} (707–715). Alternative traditions link the shrine to {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子}}, who reportedly established it during his suppression of local rebels. By the Edo period, Kōtai Shrine had significant support, including land grants and periodic reconstructions by the lords of Miyazu Domain. == Grounds == File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 本殿.JPG|'''Main Hall'''
Shinmei-zukuri with thatched roof. Features inward-curving {{ill|Chigi (architecture)|ja|千木}} and {{ill|Katsuogi|ja|鰹木}}. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 拝殿.JPG|'''Worship Hall''' File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 入口.JPG|'''Torii'''
A blackwood torii, similar to those at {{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社}} and {{ill|Nonomiya Shrine|ja|野宮神社}}. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 和泉式部歌塚.JPG|'''Izumi Shikibu Monument''' The main hall is surrounded by 83 auxiliary shrines arranged in a U-shape. * Sacred Cedar of Ryūtō * Cedar planted by {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子}} == Subsidiary Shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === Located to the left and right of the main hall: * Takuha Chichihime Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Takuha Chichihime|ja|栲幡千千姫命}} * Amano-Tajikarao Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Amano-Tajikarao|ja|天手力雄命}} === Other Shrines === * 83 shrines surrounding the main hall. == Festivals == * '''Hassaku Festival''' (September 1): Includes a procession inspired by the feudal lord’s pilgrimage, reenacted with a daimyo-style parade. == Access == ; Address * Ōe-chō Naikū, Fukuchiyama City, Kyoto Prefecture 217 ; Transportation * '''By Train:''' 15-minute walk from {{ill|Ōeyamaguchi-Naikū Station|ja|大江山口内宮駅}} on the {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道}}. * '''By Car:''' Accessible via Kyoto Prefecture Route 9. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{commons cat|Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)}} * [http://motoise-naiku.com/ Official Website of Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine] * [https://www.uminokyoto.jp/genryu/fukuchiyama/page4.html Moto-Ise Shrines - Fukuchiyama City - Sea of Kyoto] (Kyoto Northern Region Promotion Association) {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fukuchiyama]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Fukuchiyama]] [[Category:Former Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Moto-Ise]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Miyazu Domain]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]][[ja:皇大神社 (福知山市)]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:nnn]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Kotai Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:皇大神社]]

Entrance |location = {{ill|Fukuchiyama|ja|福知山市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}} Ōe-chō Naikū Aza Miyama 217 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|25|48.5|N|135|09|16.2|E|region:JP-26|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社}} |Established = Traditionally, 39th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} |Main hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} (thatch roof) |Other names = Moto-Ise Kōtai Jingū, Moto-Ise Naikū |Festival date = {{ill|April 26|en|4月26日}} |Rituals = Hassaku Parade ({{ill|September 1|en|9月1日}}) }} '''Kōtai Shrine''' (皇大神社, '''Kōtai Jinja''') is a shrine located in the Ōe district of {{ill|Fukuchiyama|ja|福知山市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Moto-Ise|ja|元伊勢}} legend as one of the temporary enshrinements of {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} before settling in {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. == Overview == Amaterasu Ōmikami, now enshrined at {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, was traditionally worshipped within the Imperial Palace. To relieve the awe of this presence, Emperor Sujin ordered the search for a suitable enshrinement location in the 6th year of his reign. After many relocations, the deity settled in Ise in the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign. The temporary enshrinement sites along this journey are remembered as the {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢}}. Kōtai Shrine is one of these Moto-Ise sites, located at the historical location known as Yoshisa-no-Miya (吉佐宮). As such, it is also referred to as {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢内宮 皇大神社|Moto-Ise Shrines=Moto-Ise Shrines|lt=Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine}}, reflecting its connection to the {{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|皇大神宮}} of Ise Grand Shrine. Other Moto-Ise shrines in the Ōe area are: {|class="wikitable" |- !colspan="3" | Moto-Ise Shrines of Fukuchiyama City |- |'''{{ill|Kotai Shrine|ja|皇大神社 (福知山市)}}''' |Ōe-chō Naikū |Associated with the Inner Shrine (Naikū) of Ise Grand Shrine |- |'''{{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社|lt=Toyouke Daijinja}}''' |Ōe-chō Tadauchi |Associated with the Outer Shrine (Gekū) of Ise Grand Shrine |- |'''{{ill|Amanoiwato_Shrine_(Fukuchiyama)|ja|天岩戸神社_(福知山市)|lt=Amanoiwato Shrine}}''' |Ōe-chō Busshōji |Kōtai Shrine’s auxiliary shrine |} Additional shrines also claim Yoshisa-no-Miya heritage, including {{ill|Kono Shrine|ja|籠神社#境外社}}, {{ill|Sasahara Shrine|ja|笶原神社}}, and {{ill|Takeno Shrine|ja|竹野神社}}. == Deity == * '''{{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}}''' - Ancestral goddess of the Imperial family and deity of Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine). == History == According to the ''{{ill|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記}}'', in the 39th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|豊鍬入姫命}} brought Amaterasu Ōmikami to Yoshisa-no-Miya in Tanba Province for four years. Kōtai Shrine is believed to stand on this site, commemorating this temporary enshrinement. The shrine's records state that it was later reconstructed during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}} (707–715). Alternative traditions link the shrine to {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子}}, who reportedly established it during his suppression of local rebels. By the Edo period, Kōtai Shrine had significant support, including land grants and periodic reconstructions by the lords of Miyazu Domain. == Grounds == File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 本殿.JPG|'''Main Hall'''
Shinmei-zukuri with thatched roof. Features inward-curving {{ill|Chigi (architecture)|ja|千木}} and {{ill|Katsuogi|ja|鰹木}}. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 拝殿.JPG|'''Worship Hall''' File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 入口.JPG|'''Torii'''
A blackwood torii, similar to those at {{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社}} and {{ill|Nonomiya Shrine|ja|野宮神社}}. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 和泉式部歌塚.JPG|'''Izumi Shikibu Monument''' The main hall is surrounded by 83 auxiliary shrines arranged in a U-shape. * Sacred Cedar of Ryūtō * Cedar planted by {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子}} == Subsidiary Shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === Located to the left and right of the main hall: * Takuha Chichihime Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Takuha Chichihime|ja|栲幡千千姫命}} * Amano-Tajikarao Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Amano-Tajikarao|ja|天手力雄命}} === Other Shrines === * 83 shrines surrounding the main hall. == Festivals == * '''Hassaku Festival''' (September 1): Includes a procession inspired by the feudal lord’s pilgrimage, reenacted with a daimyo-style parade. == Access == ; Address * Ōe-chō Naikū, Fukuchiyama City, Kyoto Prefecture 217 ; Transportation * '''By Train:''' 15-minute walk from {{ill|Ōeyamaguchi-Naikū Station|ja|大江山口内宮駅}} on the {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道}}. * '''By Car:''' Accessible via Kyoto Prefecture Route 9. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{commons cat|Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)}} * [http://motoise-naiku.com/ Official Website of Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine] * [https://www.uminokyoto.jp/genryu/fukuchiyama/page4.html Moto-Ise Shrines - Fukuchiyama City - Sea of Kyoto] (Kyoto Northern Region Promotion Association) {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fukuchiyama]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Fukuchiyama]] [[Category:Former Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Miyazu Domain]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]][[ja:皇大神社 (福知山市)]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:nnn]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Kotai Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:皇大神社]]

Kōtai Shrine is one of these Moto-Ise sites, located at the historical location known as Yoshisa-no-Miya (吉佐宮). As such, it is also referred to as {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢内宮 皇大神社|Moto-Ise Shrines=Moto-Ise Shrines|lt=Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine}}, reflecting its connection to the {{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|皇大神宮}} of Ise Grand Shrine. Other Moto-Ise shrines in the Ōe area are: {|class="wikitable" |- !colspan="3" | Moto-Ise Shrines of Fukuchiyama City |- |'''{{ill|Kotai Shrine|ja|皇大神社 (福知山市)}}''' |Ōe-chō Naikū |Associated with the Inner Shrine (Naikū) of Ise Grand Shrine |- |'''{{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社|lt=Toyouke Daijinja}}''' |Ōe-chō Tadauchi |Associated with the Outer Shrine (Gekū) of Ise Grand Shrine |- |'''{{ill|Amanoiwato_Shrine_(Fukuchiyama)|ja|天岩戸神社_(福知山市)|lt=Amanoiwato Shrine}}''' |Ōe-chō Busshōji |Kōtai Shrine’s auxiliary shrine |} Additional shrines also claim Yoshisa-no-Miya heritage, including {{ill|Kono Shrine|ja|籠神社#境外社}}, {{ill|Sasahara Shrine|ja|笶原神社}}, and {{ill|Takeno Shrine|ja|竹野神社}}. == deity == * '''{{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}}''' - Ancestral goddess of the Imperial family and deity of Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine). == History == According to the ''{{ill|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記}}'', in the 39th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|豊鍬入姫命}} brought Amaterasu Ōmikami to Yoshisa-no-Miya in Tanba Province for four years. Kōtai Shrine is believed to stand on this site, commemorating this temporary enshrinement. The shrine's records state that it was later reconstructed during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}} (707–715). Alternative traditions link the shrine to {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子}}, who reportedly established it during his suppression of local rebels. By the Edo period, Kōtai Shrine had significant support, including land grants and periodic reconstructions by the lords of Miyazu Domain. == Grounds == File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 本殿.JPG|'''Main Hall'''
Shinmei-zukuri with thatched roof. Features inward-curving {{ill|Chigi (architecture)|ja|千木}} and {{ill|Katsuogi|ja|鰹木}}. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 拝殿.JPG|'''Worship Hall''' File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 入口.JPG|'''Torii'''
A blackwood torii, similar to those at {{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社}} and {{ill|Nonomiya Shrine|ja|野宮神社}}. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 和泉式部歌塚.JPG|'''Izumi Shikibu Monument''' The main hall is surrounded by 83 auxiliary shrines arranged in a U-shape. * Sacred Cedar of Ryūtō * Cedar planted by {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子}} == Subsidiary Shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === Located to the left and right of the main hall: * Takuha Chichihime Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Takuha Chichihime|ja|栲幡千千姫命}} * Amano-Tajikarao Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Amano-Tajikarao|ja|天手力雄命}} === Other Shrines === * 83 shrines surrounding the main hall. == Festivals == * '''Hassaku Festival''' (September 1): Includes a procession inspired by the feudal lord’s pilgrimage, reenacted with a daimyo-style parade. == Access == ; Address * Ōe-chō Naikū, Fukuchiyama City, Kyoto Prefecture 217 ; Transportation * '''By Train:''' 15-minute walk from {{ill|Ōeyamaguchi-Naikū Station|ja|大江山口内宮駅}} on the {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道}}. * '''By Car:''' Accessible via Kyoto Prefecture Route 9. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{commons cat|Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)}} * [http://motoise-naiku.com/ Official Website of Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine] * [https://www.uminokyoto.jp/genryu/fukuchiyama/page4.html Moto-Ise Shrines - Fukuchiyama City - Sea of Kyoto] (Kyoto Northern Region Promotion Association) {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fukuchiyama]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Fukuchiyama]] [[Category:Former Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Miyazu Domain]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]][[ja:皇大神社 (福知山市)]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:nnn]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Kotai Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:元伊勢]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Kotai Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:皇大神社]]

It is part of the {{ill|Moto-Ise|ja|元伊勢}} legend as one of the temporary enshrinements of {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} before settling in {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. == Overview == Amaterasu Ōmikami, now enshrined at {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, was traditionally worshipped within the Imperial Palace. A blackwood torii, similar to those at {{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社}} and {{ill|Nonomiya Shrine|ja|野宮神社}}. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 和泉式部歌塚.JPG|'''Izumi Shikibu Monument''' The main hall is surrounded by 83 auxiliary shrines arranged in a U-shape. * Sacred Cedar of Ryūtō * Cedar planted by {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子}} == Subsidiary Shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === Located to the left and right of the main hall: * Takuha Chichihime Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Takuha Chichihime|ja|栲幡千千姫命}} * Amano-Tajikarao Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Amano-Tajikarao|ja|天手力雄命}} === Other Shrines === * 83 shrines surrounding the main hall. == Festivals == * '''Hassaku Festival''' (September 1): Includes a procession inspired by the feudal lord’s pilgrimage, reenacted with a daimyo-style parade. == Access == ; Address * Ōe-chō Naikū, Fukuchiyama City, Kyoto Prefecture 217 ; Transportation * '''By Train:''' 15-minute walk from {{ill|Ōeyamaguchi-Naikū Station|ja|大江山口内宮駅}} on the {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道}}. * '''By Car:''' Accessible via Kyoto Prefecture Route 9. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{commons cat|Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)}} * [http://motoise-naiku.com/ Official Website of Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine] * [https://www.uminokyoto.jp/genryu/fukuchiyama/page4.html Moto-Ise Shrines - Fukuchiyama City - Sea of Kyoto] (Kyoto Northern Region Promotion Association) {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fukuchiyama]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Fukuchiyama]] [[Category:Former Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Miyazu Domain]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]][[ja:皇大神社 (福知山市)]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:nnn]] [[Category:Kotai Shrine]][[Category:元伊勢]] [[en:Kotai Shrine]][[ja:皇大神社]]

revision.text:
{{otheruses}} [[Category:皇大神社 (福知山市)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]][[ja:皇大神社 (福知山市)]] {{Shrine |name = Kōtai Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kotaijinzya shrine.jpg|280px]]
Entrance |location = {{ill|Fukuchiyama|ja|福知山市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}} Ōe-chō Naikū Aza Miyama 217 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|25|48.5|N|135|09|16.2|E|region:JP-26|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社}} |Established = Traditionally, 39th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} |Main hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} (thatch roof) |Other names = Moto-Ise Kōtai Jingū, Moto-Ise Naikū |Festival date = {{ill|April 26|en|4月26日}} |Rituals = Hassaku Parade ({{ill|September 1|en|9月1日}}) }} '''Kōtai Shrine''' (皇大神社, '''Kōtai Jinja''') is a shrine located in the Ōe district of {{ill|Fukuchiyama|ja|福知山市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Moto-Ise|ja|元伊勢}} legend as one of the temporary enshrinements of {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} before settling in {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. == Overview == Amaterasu Ōmikami, now enshrined at {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, was traditionally worshipped within the Imperial Palace. To relieve the awe of this presence, Emperor Sujin ordered the search for a suitable enshrinement location in the 6th year of his reign. After many relocations, the deity settled in Ise in the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign. The temporary enshrinement sites along this journey are remembered as the {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢}}. Kōtai Shrine is one of these Moto-Ise sites, located at the historical location known as Yoshisa-no-Miya (吉佐宮). As such, it is also referred to as {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢内宮 皇大神社|Moto-Ise Shrines=Moto-Ise Shrines|lt=Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine}}, reflecting its connection to the {{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|皇大神宮}} of Ise Grand Shrine. Other Moto-Ise shrines in the Ōe area are: {|class="wikitable" |- !colspan="3" | Moto-Ise Shrines of Fukuchiyama City |- |'''{{ill|Kotai Shrine|ja|皇大神社 (福知山市)}}''' |Ōe-chō Naikū |Associated with the Inner Shrine (Naikū) of Ise Grand Shrine |- |'''{{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社|lt=Toyouke Daijinja}}''' |Ōe-chō Tadauchi |Associated with the Outer Shrine (Gekū) of Ise Grand Shrine |- |'''{{ill|Amanoiwato_Shrine_(Fukuchiyama)|ja|天岩戸神社_(福知山市)|lt=Amanoiwato Shrine}}''' |Ōe-chō Busshōji |Kōtai Shrine’s auxiliary shrine |} Additional shrines also claim Yoshisa-no-Miya heritage, including {{ill|Kono Shrine|ja|籠神社#境外社}}, {{ill|Sasahara Shrine|ja|笶原神社}}, and {{ill|Takeno Shrine|ja|竹野神社}}. == deity == * '''{{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}}''' - Ancestral goddess of the Imperial family and deity of Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine). == History == According to the ''{{ill|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記}}'', in the 39th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|豊鍬入姫命}} brought Amaterasu Ōmikami to Yoshisa-no-Miya in Tanba Province for four years. Kōtai Shrine is believed to stand on this site, commemorating this temporary enshrinement. The shrine's records state that it was later reconstructed during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}} (707–715). Alternative traditions link the shrine to {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子}}, who reportedly established it during his suppression of local rebels. By the Edo period, Kōtai Shrine had significant support, including land grants and periodic reconstructions by the lords of Miyazu Domain. == Grounds == File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 本殿.JPG|'''Main Hall'''
Shinmei-zukuri with thatched roof. Features inward-curving {{ill|Chigi (architecture)|ja|千木}} and {{ill|Katsuogi|ja|鰹木}}. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 拝殿.JPG|'''Worship Hall''' File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 入口.JPG|'''Torii'''
A blackwood torii, similar to those at {{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社}} and {{ill|Nonomiya Shrine|ja|野宮神社}}. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 和泉式部歌塚.JPG|'''Izumi Shikibu Monument''' The main hall is surrounded by 83 auxiliary shrines arranged in a U-shape. * Sacred Cedar of Ryūtō * Cedar planted by {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子}} == Subsidiary Shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === Located to the left and right of the main hall: * Takuha Chichihime Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Takuha Chichihime|ja|栲幡千千姫命}} * Amano-Tajikarao Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Amano-Tajikarao|ja|天手力雄命}} === Other Shrines === * 83 shrines surrounding the main hall. == Festivals == * '''Hassaku Festival''' (September 1): Includes a procession inspired by the feudal lord’s pilgrimage, reenacted with a daimyo-style parade. == Access == ; Address * Ōe-chō Naikū, Fukuchiyama City, Kyoto Prefecture 217 ; Transportation * '''By Train:''' 15-minute walk from {{ill|Ōeyamaguchi-Naikū Station|ja|大江山口内宮駅}} on the {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道}}. * '''By Car:''' Accessible via Kyoto Prefecture Route 9. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{commons cat|Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)}} * [http://motoise-naiku.com/ Official Website of Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine] * [https://www.uminokyoto.jp/genryu/fukuchiyama/page4.html Moto-Ise Shrines - Fukuchiyama City - Sea of Kyoto] (Kyoto Northern Region Promotion Association) {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fukuchiyama]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Fukuchiyama]] [[Category:Former Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Miyazu Domain]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]][[ja:皇大神社 (福知山市)]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:nnn]] [[Category:Kotai Shrine]][[Category:元伊勢]] [[en:Kotai Shrine]][[ja:皇大神社]]

Address * Ōe-chō Naikū, Fukuchiyama City, Kyoto Prefecture 217 ; Transportation * '''By Train:''' 15-minute walk from {{ill|Ōeyamaguchi-Naikū Station|ja|大江山口内宮駅}} on the {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道}}. * '''By Car:''' Accessible via Kyoto Prefecture Route 9. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{commons cat|Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)}} * [http://motoise-naiku.com/ Official Website of Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine] * [https://www.uminokyoto.jp/genryu/fukuchiyama/page4.html Moto-Ise Shrines - Fukuchiyama City - Sea of Kyoto] (Kyoto Northern Region Promotion Association) {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fukuchiyama]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Fukuchiyama]] [[Category:Former Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Miyazu Domain]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]][[ja:皇大神社 (福知山市)]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:nnn]] [[Category:Kotai Shrine]][[en:Kotai Shrine]][[ja:皇大神社]]

revision.text:
{{otheruses}} [[Category:皇大神社 (福知山市)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]][[ja:皇大神社 (福知山市)]] {{Shrine |name = Kōtai Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kotaijinzya shrine.jpg|280px]]
Entrance |location = {{ill|Fukuchiyama|ja|福知山市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}} Ōe-chō Naikū Aza Miyama 217 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|25|48.5|N|135|09|16.2|E|region:JP-26|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社}} |Established = Traditionally, 39th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} |Main hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} (thatch roof) |Other names = Moto-Ise Kōtai Jingū, Moto-Ise Naikū |Festival date = {{ill|April 26|en|4月26日}} |Rituals = Hassaku Parade ({{ill|September 1|en|9月1日}}) }} '''Kōtai Shrine''' (皇大神社, '''Kōtai Jinja''') is a shrine located in the Ōe district of {{ill|Fukuchiyama|ja|福知山市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Moto-Ise|ja|元伊勢}} legend as one of the temporary enshrinements of {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} before settling in {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. == Overview == Amaterasu Ōmikami, now enshrined at {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, was traditionally worshipped within the Imperial Palace. To relieve the awe of this presence, Emperor Sujin ordered the search for a suitable enshrinement location in the 6th year of his reign. After many relocations, the deity settled in Ise in the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign. The temporary enshrinement sites along this journey are remembered as the {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢}}. Kōtai Shrine is one of these Moto-Ise sites, located at the historical location known as Yoshisa-no-Miya (吉佐宮). As such, it is also referred to as {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢内宮 皇大神社|Moto-Ise Shrines=Moto-Ise Shrines|lt=Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine}}, reflecting its connection to the {{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|皇大神宮}} of Ise Grand Shrine. Other Moto-Ise shrines in the Ōe area are: {|class="wikitable" |- !colspan="3" | Moto-Ise Shrines of Fukuchiyama City |- |'''{{ill|Kotai Shrine|ja|皇大神社 (福知山市)}}''' |Ōe-chō Naikū |Associated with the Inner Shrine (Naikū) of Ise Grand Shrine |- |'''{{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社|lt=Toyouke Daijinja}}''' |Ōe-chō Tadauchi |Associated with the Outer Shrine (Gekū) of Ise Grand Shrine |- |'''{{ill|Amanoiwato_Shrine_(Fukuchiyama)|ja|天岩戸神社_(福知山市)|lt=Amanoiwato Shrine}}''' |Ōe-chō Busshōji |Kōtai Shrine’s auxiliary shrine |} Additional shrines also claim Yoshisa-no-Miya heritage, including {{ill|Kono Shrine|ja|籠神社#境外社}}, {{ill|Sasahara Shrine|ja|笶原神社}}, and {{ill|Takeno Shrine|ja|竹野神社}}. == deity == * '''{{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}}''' - Ancestral goddess of the Imperial family and deity of Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine). == History == According to the ''{{ill|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記}}'', in the 39th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|豊鍬入姫命}} brought Amaterasu Ōmikami to Yoshisa-no-Miya in Tanba Province for four years. Kōtai Shrine is believed to stand on this site, commemorating this temporary enshrinement. The shrine's records state that it was later reconstructed during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}} (707–715). Alternative traditions link the shrine to {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子}}, who reportedly established it during his suppression of local rebels. By the Edo period, Kōtai Shrine had significant support, including land grants and periodic reconstructions by the lords of Miyazu Domain. == Grounds == File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 本殿.JPG|'''Main Hall'''
Shinmei-zukuri with thatched roof. Features inward-curving {{ill|Chigi (architecture)|ja|千木}} and {{ill|Katsuogi|ja|鰹木}}. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 拝殿.JPG|'''Worship Hall''' File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 入口.JPG|'''Torii'''
A blackwood torii, similar to those at {{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社}} and {{ill|Nonomiya Shrine|ja|野宮神社}}. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 和泉式部歌塚.JPG|'''Izumi Shikibu Monument''' The main hall is surrounded by 83 auxiliary shrines arranged in a U-shape. * Sacred Cedar of Ryūtō * Cedar planted by {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子}} == Subsidiary Shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === Located to the left and right of the main hall: * Takuha Chichihime Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Takuha Chichihime|ja|栲幡千千姫命}} * Amano-Tajikarao Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Amano-Tajikarao|ja|天手力雄命}} === Other Shrines === * 83 shrines surrounding the main hall. == Festivals == * '''Hassaku Festival''' (September 1): Includes a procession inspired by the feudal lord’s pilgrimage, reenacted with a daimyo-style parade. == Access == ; Address * Ōe-chō Naikū, Fukuchiyama City, Kyoto Prefecture 217 ; Transportation * '''By Train:''' 15-minute walk from {{ill|Ōeyamaguchi-Naikū Station|ja|大江山口内宮駅}} on the {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道}}. * '''By Car:''' Accessible via Kyoto Prefecture Route 9. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{commons cat|Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)}} * [http://motoise-naiku.com/ Official Website of Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine] * [https://www.uminokyoto.jp/genryu/fukuchiyama/page4.html Moto-Ise Shrines - Fukuchiyama City - Sea of Kyoto] (Kyoto Northern Region Promotion Association) {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fukuchiyama]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Fukuchiyama]] [[Category:Former Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Miyazu Domain]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]][[ja:皇大神社 (福知山市)]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:nnn]] [[Category:Kotai Shrine]][[en:Kotai Shrine]][[ja:皇大神社]]

{{otheruses}} [[Category:皇大神社 (福知山市)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]][[ja:皇大神社 (福知山市)]] {{Shrine |name = Kōtai Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kotaijinzya shrine.jpg|280px]]
Entrance |location = {{ill|Fukuchiyama|ja|福知山市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}} Ōe-chō Naikū Aza Miyama 217 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|25|48.5|N|135|09|16.2|E|region:JP-26|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社}} |Established = Traditionally, 39th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} |Main hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} (thatch roof) |Other names = Moto-Ise Kōtai Jingū, Moto-Ise Naikū |Festival date = {{ill|April 26|en|4月26日}} |Rituals = Hassaku Parade ({{ill|September 1|en|9月1日}}) }} '''Kōtai Shrine''' (皇大神社, '''Kōtai Jinja''') is a shrine located in the Ōe district of {{ill|Fukuchiyama|ja|福知山市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Moto-Ise|ja|元伊勢}} legend as one of the temporary enshrinements of {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} before settling in {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. == Overview == Amaterasu Ōmikami, now enshrined at {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, was traditionally worshipped within the Imperial Palace. To relieve the awe of this presence, Emperor Sujin ordered the search for a suitable enshrinement location in the 6th year of his reign. After many relocations, the deity settled in Ise in the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign. The temporary enshrinement sites along this journey are remembered as the {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢}}. Kōtai Shrine is one of these Moto-Ise sites, located at the historical location known as Yoshisa-no-Miya (吉佐宮). As such, it is also referred to as {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢内宮 皇大神社|Moto-Ise Shrines=Moto-Ise Shrines|lt=Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine}}, reflecting its connection to the {{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|皇大神宮}} of Ise Grand Shrine. Other Moto-Ise shrines in the Ōe area are: {|class="wikitable" |- !colspan="3" | Moto-Ise Shrines of Fukuchiyama City |- |'''{{ill|Kotai Shrine|ja|皇大神社 (福知山市)}}''' |Ōe-chō Naikū |Associated with the Inner Shrine (Naikū) of Ise Grand Shrine |- |'''{{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社|lt=Toyouke Daijinja}}''' |Ōe-chō Tadauchi |Associated with the Outer Shrine (Gekū) of Ise Grand Shrine |- |'''{{ill|Amanoiwato_Shrine_(Fukuchiyama)|ja|天岩戸神社_(福知山市)|lt=Amanoiwato Shrine}}''' |Ōe-chō Busshōji |Kōtai Shrine’s auxiliary shrine |} Additional shrines also claim Yoshisa-no-Miya heritage, including {{ill|Kono Shrine|ja|籠神社#境外社}}, {{ill|Sasahara Shrine|ja|笶原神社}}, and {{ill|Takeno Shrine|ja|竹野神社}}. == deity == * '''{{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}}''' - Ancestral goddess of the Imperial family and deity of Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine). == History == According to the ''{{ill|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記}}'', in the 39th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|豊鍬入姫命}} brought Amaterasu Ōmikami to Yoshisa-no-Miya in Tanba Province for four years. Kōtai Shrine is believed to stand on this site, commemorating this temporary enshrinement. The shrine's records state that it was later reconstructed during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}} (707–715). Alternative traditions link the shrine to {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子}}, who reportedly established it during his suppression of local rebels. By the Edo period, Kōtai Shrine had significant support, including land grants and periodic reconstructions by the lords of Miyazu Domain. == Grounds == File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 本殿.JPG|'''Main Hall'''
Shinmei-zukuri with thatched roof. Features inward-curving {{ill|Chigi (architecture)|ja|千木}} and {{ill|Katsuogi|ja|鰹木}}. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 拝殿.JPG|'''Worship Hall''' File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 入口.JPG|'''Torii'''
A blackwood torii, similar to those at {{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社}} and {{ill|Nonomiya Shrine|ja|野宮神社}}. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 和泉式部歌塚.JPG|'''Izumi Shikibu Monument''' The main hall is surrounded by 83 auxiliary shrines arranged in a U-shape. * Sacred Cedar of Ryūtō * Cedar planted by {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子}} == Subsidiary Shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === Located to the left and right of the main hall: * Takuha Chichihime Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Takuha Chichihime|ja|栲幡千千姫命}} * Amano-Tajikarao Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Amano-Tajikarao|ja|天手力雄命}} === Other Shrines === * 83 shrines surrounding the main hall. == Festivals == * '''Hassaku Festival''' (September 1): Includes a procession inspired by the feudal lord’s pilgrimage, reenacted with a daimyo-style parade. == Access == ; Address * Ōe-chō Naikū, Fukuchiyama City, Kyoto Prefecture 217 ; Transportation * '''By Train:''' 15-minute walk from {{ill|Ōeyamaguchi-Naikū Station|ja|大江山口内宮駅}} on the {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道}}. * '''By Car:''' Accessible via Kyoto Prefecture Route 9. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{commons cat|Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)}} * [http://motoise-naiku.com/ Official Website of Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine] * [https://www.uminokyoto.jp/genryu/fukuchiyama/page4.html Moto-Ise Shrines - Fukuchiyama City - Sea of Kyoto] (Kyoto Northern Region Promotion Association) {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fukuchiyama]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Fukuchiyama]] [[Category:Former Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Miyazu Domain]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]][[ja:皇大神社 (福知山市)]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:nnn]] [[Category:Kotai Shrine]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[en:Kotai Shrine]][[ja:皇大神社]]

{{otheruses}} [[Category:皇大神社 (福知山市)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]][[ja:皇大神社 (福知山市)]] {{Shrine |name = Kōtai Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kotaijinzya shrine.jpg|280px]]
Entrance |location = {{ill|Fukuchiyama|ja|福知山市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}} Ōe-chō Naikū Aza Miyama 217 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|25|48.5|N|135|09|16.2|E|region:JP-26|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社}} |Established = Traditionally, 39th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} |Main hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} (thatch roof) |Other names = Moto-Ise Kōtai Jingū, Moto-Ise Naikū |Festival date = {{ill|April 26|en|4月26日}} |Rituals = Hassaku Parade ({{ill|September 1|en|9月1日}}) }} '''Kōtai Shrine''' (皇大神社, '''Kōtai Jinja''') is a shrine located in the Ōe district of {{ill|Fukuchiyama|ja|福知山市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Moto-Ise|ja|元伊勢}} legend as one of the temporary enshrinements of {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} before settling in {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. == Overview == Amaterasu Ōmikami, now enshrined at {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, was traditionally worshipped within the Imperial Palace. To relieve the awe of this presence, Emperor Sujin ordered the search for a suitable enshrinement location in the 6th year of his reign. After many relocations, the deity settled in Ise in the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign. The temporary enshrinement sites along this journey are remembered as the {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢}}. Kōtai Shrine is one of these Moto-Ise sites, located at the historical location known as Yoshisa-no-Miya (吉佐宮). As such, it is also referred to as {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢内宮 皇大神社|Moto-Ise Shrines=Moto-Ise Shrines|lt=Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine}}, reflecting its connection to the {{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|皇大神宮}} of Ise Grand Shrine. Other Moto-Ise shrines in the Ōe area are: {|class="wikitable" |- !colspan="3" | Moto-Ise Shrines of Fukuchiyama City |- |'''{{ill|Kotai Shrine|ja|皇大神社 (福知山市)}}''' |Ōe-chō Naikū |Associated with the Inner Shrine (Naikū) of Ise Grand Shrine |- |'''{{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社|lt=Toyouke Daijinja}}''' |Ōe-chō Tadauchi |Associated with the Outer Shrine (Gekū) of Ise Grand Shrine |- |'''{{ill|Amanoiwato_Shrine_(Fukuchiyama)|ja|天岩戸神社_(福知山市)|lt=Amanoiwato Shrine}}''' |Ōe-chō Busshōji |Kōtai Shrine’s auxiliary shrine |} Additional shrines also claim Yoshisa-no-Miya heritage, including {{ill|Kono Shrine|ja|籠神社#境外社}}, {{ill|Sasahara Shrine|ja|笶原神社}}, and {{ill|Takeno Shrine|ja|竹野神社}}. == deity == * '''{{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}}''' - Ancestral goddess of the Imperial family and deity of Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine). == History == According to the ''{{ill|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記}}'', in the 39th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|豊鍬入姫命}} brought Amaterasu Ōmikami to Yoshisa-no-Miya in Tanba Province for four years. Kōtai Shrine is believed to stand on this site, commemorating this temporary enshrinement. The shrine's records state that it was later reconstructed during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}} (707–715). Alternative traditions link the shrine to {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子}}, who reportedly established it during his suppression of local rebels. By the Edo period, Kōtai Shrine had significant support, including land grants and periodic reconstructions by the lords of Miyazu Domain. == Grounds == File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 本殿.JPG|'''Main Hall'''
Shinmei-zukuri with thatched roof. Features inward-curving {{ill|Chigi (architecture)|ja|千木}} and {{ill|Katsuogi|ja|鰹木}}. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 拝殿.JPG|'''Worship Hall''' File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 入口.JPG|'''Torii'''
A blackwood torii, similar to those at {{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社}} and {{ill|Nonomiya Shrine|ja|野宮神社}}. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 和泉式部歌塚.JPG|'''Izumi Shikibu Monument''' The main hall is surrounded by 83 auxiliary shrines arranged in a U-shape. * Sacred Cedar of Ryūtō * Cedar planted by {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子}} == Subsidiary Shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === Located to the left and right of the main hall: * Takuha Chichihime Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Takuha Chichihime|ja|栲幡千千姫命}} * Amano-Tajikarao Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Amano-Tajikarao|ja|天手力雄命}} === Other Shrines === * 83 shrines surrounding the main hall. == Festivals == * '''Hassaku Festival''' (September 1): Includes a procession inspired by the feudal lord’s pilgrimage, reenacted with a daimyo-style parade. == Access == ; Address * Ōe-chō Naikū, Fukuchiyama City, Kyoto Prefecture 217 ; Transportation * '''By Train:''' 15-minute walk from {{ill|Ōeyamaguchi-Naikū Station|ja|大江山口内宮駅}} on the {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道}}. * '''By Car:''' Accessible via Kyoto Prefecture Route 9. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{commons cat|Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)}} * [http://motoise-naiku.com/ Official Website of Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine] * [https://www.uminokyoto.jp/genryu/fukuchiyama/page4.html Moto-Ise Shrines - Fukuchiyama City - Sea of Kyoto] (Kyoto Northern Region Promotion Association) {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fukuchiyama]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Fukuchiyama]] [[Category:Former Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Miyazu Domain]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]][[ja:皇大神社 (福知山市)]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:nnn]] [[Category:Kotai Shrine]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]][[sn:Kōtai Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kōtai Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kōtai Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]][[sn:Kōtai Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kōtai Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kōtai Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]][[sn:Kōtai Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kōtai Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kōtai Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kōtai Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Kotai Shrine]][[ja:皇大神社]]

{{otheruses}} [[Category:皇大神社 (福知山市)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]][[ja:皇大神社 (福知山市)]] {{Shrine |name = Kōtai Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kotaijinzya shrine.jpg|280px]]
Entrance |location = {{ill|Fukuchiyama|ja|福知山市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}} Ōe-chō Naikū Aza Miyama 217 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|25|48.5|N|135|09|16.2|E|region:JP-26|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社}} |Established = Traditionally, 39th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} |Main hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} (thatch roof) |Other names = Moto-Ise Kōtai Jingū, Moto-Ise Naikū |Festival date = {{ill|April 26|en|4月26日}} |Rituals = Hassaku Parade ({{ill|September 1|en|9月1日}}) }} '''Kōtai Shrine''' (皇大神社, '''Kōtai Jinja''') is a shrine located in the Ōe district of {{ill|Fukuchiyama|ja|福知山市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Moto-Ise|ja|元伊勢}} legend as one of the temporary enshrinements of {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} before settling in {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. == Overview == Amaterasu Ōmikami, now enshrined at {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, was traditionally worshipped within the Imperial Palace. To relieve the awe of this presence, Emperor Sujin ordered the search for a suitable enshrinement location in the 6th year of his reign. After many relocations, the deity settled in Ise in the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign. The temporary enshrinement sites along this journey are remembered as the {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢}}. Kōtai Shrine is one of these Moto-Ise sites, located at the historical location known as Yoshisa-no-Miya (吉佐宮). As such, it is also referred to as {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢内宮 皇大神社|Moto-Ise Shrines=Moto-Ise Shrines|lt=Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine}}, reflecting its connection to the {{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|皇大神宮}} of Ise Grand Shrine. Other Moto-Ise shrines in the Ōe area are: {|class="wikitable" |- !colspan="3" | Moto-Ise Shrines of Fukuchiyama City |- |'''{{ill|Kotai Shrine|ja|皇大神社 (福知山市)}}''' |Ōe-chō Naikū |Associated with the Inner Shrine (Naikū) of Ise Grand Shrine |- |'''{{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社|lt=Toyouke Daijinja}}''' |Ōe-chō Tadauchi |Associated with the Outer Shrine (Gekū) of Ise Grand Shrine |- |'''{{ill|Amanoiwato_Shrine_(Fukuchiyama)|ja|天岩戸神社_(福知山市)|lt=Amanoiwato Shrine}}''' |Ōe-chō Busshōji |Kōtai Shrine’s auxiliary shrine |} Additional shrines also claim Yoshisa-no-Miya heritage, including {{ill|Kono Shrine|ja|籠神社#境外社}}, {{ill|Sasahara Shrine|ja|笶原神社}}, and {{ill|Takeno Shrine|ja|竹野神社}}. == deity == * '''{{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}}''' - Ancestral goddess of the Imperial family and deity of Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine). == History == According to the ''{{ill|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記}}'', in the 39th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|豊鍬入姫命}} brought Amaterasu Ōmikami to Yoshisa-no-Miya in Tanba Province for four years. Kōtai Shrine is believed to stand on this site, commemorating this temporary enshrinement. The shrine's records state that it was later reconstructed during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}} (707–715). Alternative traditions link the shrine to {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子}}, who reportedly established it during his suppression of local rebels. By the Edo period, Kōtai Shrine had significant support, including land grants and periodic reconstructions by the lords of Miyazu Domain. == Grounds == File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 本殿.JPG|'''Main Hall'''
Shinmei-zukuri with thatched roof. Features inward-curving {{ill|Chigi (architecture)|ja|千木}} and {{ill|Katsuogi|ja|鰹木}}. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 拝殿.JPG|'''Worship Hall''' File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 入口.JPG|'''Torii'''
A blackwood torii, similar to those at {{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社}} and {{ill|Nonomiya Shrine|ja|野宮神社}}. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 和泉式部歌塚.JPG|'''Izumi Shikibu Monument''' The main hall is surrounded by 83 auxiliary shrines arranged in a U-shape. * Sacred Cedar of Ryūtō * Cedar planted by {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子}} == Subsidiary Shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === Located to the left and right of the main hall: * Takuha Chichihime Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Takuha Chichihime|ja|栲幡千千姫命}} * Amano-Tajikarao Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Amano-Tajikarao|ja|天手力雄命}} === Other Shrines === * 83 shrines surrounding the main hall. == Festivals == * '''Hassaku Festival''' (September 1): Includes a procession inspired by the feudal lord’s pilgrimage, reenacted with a daimyo-style parade. == Access == ; Address * Ōe-chō Naikū, Fukuchiyama City, Kyoto Prefecture 217 ; Transportation * '''By Train:''' 15-minute walk from {{ill|Ōeyamaguchi-Naikū Station|ja|大江山口内宮駅}} on the {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道}}. * '''By Car:''' Accessible via Kyoto Prefecture Route 9. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{commons cat|Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)}} * [http://motoise-naiku.com/ Official Website of Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine] * [https://www.uminokyoto.jp/genryu/fukuchiyama/page4.html Moto-Ise Shrines -
