Kōtai Shrine is believed to stand on this site, commemorating this temporary enshrinement. The shrine's records state that it was later reconstructed during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇|12=draft}} (707–715). Alternative traditions link the shrine to {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子|12=draft}}, who reportedly established it during his suppression of local rebels. By the Edo period, Kōtai Shrine had significant support, including land grants and periodic reconstructions by the lords of Miyazu Domain. == Grounds == File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 本殿.JPG|'''Main Hall'''
Shinmei-zukuri with thatched roof. Features inward-curving {{ill|Chigi (architecture)|ja|千木|12=draft}} and {{ill|Katsuogi|ja|鰹木|12=draft}}. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 拝殿.JPG|'''Worship Hall''' File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 入口.JPG|'''Torii'''
A blackwood torii, similar to those at {{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社|12=draft}} and {{ill|Nonomiya Shrine|ja|野宮神社|12=draft}}. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 和泉式部歌塚.JPG|'''Izumi Shikibu Monument''' The main hall is surrounded by 83 auxiliary shrines arranged in a U-shape. * Sacred Cedar of Ryūtō * Cedar planted by {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子|12=draft}} == Subsidiary Shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === Located to the left and right of the main hall: * Takuha Chichihime Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Takuha Chichihime|ja|栲幡千千姫命|1=Takuhadachiji-hime|12=draft}} * Amano-Tajikarao Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Amano-Tajikarao|ja|天手力雄命|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao|12=draft}} === Other Shrines === * 83 shrines surrounding the main hall. == Festivals == * '''Hassaku Festival''' (September 1): Includes a procession inspired by the feudal lord’s pilgrimage, reenacted with a daimyo-style parade. == Access == ; Address * Ōe-chō Naikū, Fukuchiyama City, Kyoto Prefecture 217 ; Transportation * '''By Train:''' 15-minute walk from {{ill|Ōeyamaguchi-Naikū Station|ja|大江山口内宮駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道|12=draft}}. * '''By Car:''' Accessible via Kyoto Prefecture Route 9. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{commons cat|Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)}} * [http://motoise-naiku.com/ Official Website of Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine] * [https://www.uminokyoto.jp/genryu/fukuchiyama/page4.html Moto-Ise Shrines - Fukuchiyama City - Sea of Kyoto] (Kyoto Northern Region Promotion Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:皇大神社 (福知山市)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category: tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fukuchiyama]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Fukuchiyama]] [[Category:Former Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Miyazu Domain]] [[Category:Kotai Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kōtai Shrine]] [[ja:皇大神社 (福知山市)]] [[en:Kotai Shrine]] [[ja:皇大神社]]

{{otheruses}} {{Shrine |name = Kōtai Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kotaijinzya shrine.jpg|280px]]
Entrance |location = {{ill|Fukuchiyama|ja|福知山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}} Ōe-chō Naikū Aza Miyama 217 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|25|48.5|N|135|09|16.2|E|region:JP-26|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|12=draft}} |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社|12=draft}} |Established = Traditionally, 39th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft}} |Main hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft}} (thatch roof) |Other names = Moto-Ise Kōtai Jingū, Moto-Ise Naikū |Festival date = {{ill|April 26|en|4月26日|12=draft}} |Rituals = Hassaku Parade ({{ill|September 1|en|9月1日|12=draft}}) }} '''Kōtai Shrine''' (皇大神社, '''Kōtai Jinja''') is a shrine located in the Ōe district of {{ill|Fukuchiyama|ja|福知山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Moto-Ise|ja|元伊勢|12=draft}} legend as one of the temporary enshrinements of {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|12=draft}} before settling in {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft}}. == Overview == Amaterasu Ōmikami, now enshrined at {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft}}, was traditionally worshipped within the Imperial Palace. To relieve the awe of this presence, Emperor Sujin ordered the search for a suitable enshrinement location in the 6th year of his reign. After many relocations, the deity settled in Ise in the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign. The temporary enshrinement sites along this journey are remembered as the {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢|12=draft}}. Kōtai Shrine is one of these Moto-Ise sites, located at the historical location known as Yoshisa-no-Miya (吉佐宮). As such, it is also referred to as {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢内宮 皇大神社|Moto-Ise Shrines=Moto-Ise Shrines|lt=Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine|12=draft}}, reflecting its connection to the {{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}} of Ise Grand Shrine. Other Moto-Ise shrines in the Ōe area are: {|class="wikitable" |- !colspan="3" | {{ill|Three Moto-Ise Shrines of Fukuchiyama|ja|元伊勢三社|12=draft}} |- |'''{{ill|Kotai Shrine|ja|皇大神社 (福知山市)|12=draft}}''' |Ōe-chō Naikū |Associated with the Inner Shrine (Naikū) of Ise Grand Shrine |- |'''{{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社|lt=Toyouke Daijinja|12=draft}}''' |Ōe-chō Tadauchi |Associated with the Outer Shrine (Gekū) of Ise Grand Shrine |- |'''{{ill|Amanoiwato_Shrine_(Fukuchiyama)|ja|天岩戸神社_(福知山市)|lt=Amanoiwato Shrine|12=draft}}''' |Ōe-chō Busshōji |Kōtai Shrine’s auxiliary shrine |} Additional shrines also claim Yoshisa-no-Miya heritage, including {{ill|Kono Shrine|ja|籠神社#境外社|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sasahara Shrine|ja|笶原神社|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Takeno Shrine|ja|竹野神社|12=draft}}. == deity == * '''{{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|12=draft}}''' - Ancestral goddess of the Imperial family and deity of Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine). == History == According to the ''{{ill|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|12=draft}}'', in the 39th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft}}, {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|12=draft}} brought Amaterasu Ōmikami to Yoshisa-no-Miya in Tanba Province for four years. Kōtai Shrine is believed to stand on this site, commemorating this temporary enshrinement. The shrine's records state that it was later reconstructed during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇|12=draft}} (707–715). Alternative traditions link the shrine to {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子|12=draft}}, who reportedly established it during his suppression of local rebels. By the Edo period, Kōtai Shrine had significant support, including land grants and periodic reconstructions by the lords of Miyazu Domain. == Grounds == File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 本殿.JPG|'''Main Hall'''
Shinmei-zukuri with thatched roof. Features inward-curving {{ill|Chigi (architecture)|ja|千木|12=draft}} and {{ill|Katsuogi|ja|鰹木|12=draft}}. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 拝殿.JPG|'''Worship Hall''' File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 入口.JPG|'''Torii'''
A blackwood torii, similar to those at {{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社|12=draft}} and {{ill|Nonomiya Shrine|ja|野宮神社|12=draft}}. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 和泉式部歌塚.JPG|'''Izumi Shikibu Monument''' The main hall is surrounded by 83 auxiliary shrines arranged in a U-shape. * Sacred Cedar of Ryūtō * Cedar planted by {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子|12=draft}} == Subsidiary Shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === Located to the left and right of the main hall: * Takuha Chichihime Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Takuha Chichihime|ja|栲幡千千姫命|1=Takuhadachiji-hime|12=draft}} * Amano-Tajikarao Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Amano-Tajikarao|ja|天手力雄命|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao|12=draft}} === Other Shrines === * 83 shrines surrounding the main hall. == Festivals == * '''Hassaku Festival''' (September 1): Includes a procession inspired by the feudal lord’s pilgrimage, reenacted with a daimyo-style parade. == Access == ; Address * Ōe-chō Naikū, Fukuchiyama City, Kyoto Prefecture 217 ; Transportation * '''By Train:''' 15-minute walk from {{ill|Ōeyamaguchi-Naikū Station|ja|大江山口内宮駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道|12=draft}}. * '''By Car:''' Accessible via Kyoto Prefecture Route 9. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{commons cat|Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)}} * [http://motoise-naiku.com/ Official Website of Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine] * [https://www.uminokyoto.jp/genryu/fukuchiyama/page4.html Moto-Ise Shrines - Fukuchiyama City - Sea of Kyoto] (Kyoto Northern Region Promotion Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:皇大神社 (福知山市)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fukuchiyama]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Fukuchiyama]] [[Category:Former Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Miyazu Domain]] [[Category:Kotai Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kōtai Shrine]] [[ja:皇大神社 (福知山市)]] [[en:Kotai Shrine]] [[ja:皇大神社]]

Shinmei-zukuri with thatched roof. Features inward-curving {{ill|Chigi (architecture)|ja|千木|12=draft}} and {{ill|Katsuogi|ja|鰹木|12=draft}}. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 拝殿.JPG|'''Worship Hall''' File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 入口.JPG|'''Torii'''
A blackwood torii, similar to those at {{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社|12=draft}} and {{ill|Nonomiya Shrine|ja|野宮神社|12=draft}}. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 和泉式部歌塚.JPG|'''Izumi Shikibu Monument''' The main hall is surrounded by 83 auxiliary shrines arranged in a U-shape. * Sacred Cedar of Ryūtō * Cedar planted by {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子|12=draft}} == Subsidiary Shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === Located to the left and right of the main hall: * Takuha Chichihime Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Takuha Chichihime|ja|栲幡千千姫命|1=Takuhadachiji-hime|12=draft}} * Amano-Tajikarao Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Amano-Tajikarao|ja|天手力雄命|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao|12=draft}} === Other Shrines === * 83 shrines surrounding the main hall. == Festivals == * '''Hassaku Festival''' (September 1): Includes a procession inspired by the feudal lord’s pilgrimage, reenacted with a daimyo-style parade. == Access == ; Address * Ōe-chō Naikū, Fukuchiyama City, Kyoto Prefecture 217 ; Transportation * '''By Train:''' 15-minute walk from {{ill|Ōeyamaguchi-Naikū Station|ja|大江山口内宮駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道|12=draft}}. * '''By Car:''' Accessible via Kyoto Prefecture Route 9. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{commons cat|Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)}} * [http://motoise-naiku.com/ Official Website of Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine] * [https://www.uminokyoto.jp/genryu/fukuchiyama/page4.html Moto-Ise Shrines - Fukuchiyama City - Sea of Kyoto] (Kyoto Northern Region Promotion Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:皇大神社 (福知山市)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fukuchiyama]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Fukuchiyama]] [[Category:Former Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Miyazu Domain]] [[Category:Kotai Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kōtai Shrine]] [[ja:皇大神社 (福知山市)]] [[en:Kotai Shrine]] [[ja:皇大神社]]

{{otheruses}} {{Shrine |name = Kōtai Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kotaijinzya shrine.jpg|280px]]
Entrance |location = {{ill|Fukuchiyama|ja|福知山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}} Ōe-chō Naikū Aza Miyama 217 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|25|48.5|N|135|09|16.2|E|region:JP-26|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|12=draft}} |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社|12=draft}} |Established = Traditionally, 39th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft}} |Main hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft}} (thatch roof) |Other names = Moto-Ise Kōtai Jingū, Moto-Ise Naikū |Festival date = {{ill|April 26|en|4月26日|12=draft}} |Rituals = Hassaku Parade ({{ill|September 1|en|9月1日|12=draft}}) }} '''Kōtai Shrine''' (皇大神社, '''Kōtai Jinja''') is a shrine located in the Ōe district of {{ill|Fukuchiyama|ja|福知山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Moto-Ise|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|12=draft}} legend as one of the temporary enshrinements of {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|12=draft}} before settling in {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft}}. == Overview == Amaterasu Ōmikami, now enshrined at {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft}}, was traditionally worshipped within the Imperial Palace. To relieve the awe of this presence, Emperor Sujin ordered the search for a suitable enshrinement location in the 6th year of his reign. After many relocations, the deity settled in Ise in the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign. The temporary enshrinement sites along this journey are remembered as the {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|12=draft}}. Kōtai Shrine is one of these Moto-Ise sites, located at the historical location known as Yoshisa-no-Miya (吉佐宮). As such, it is also referred to as {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢内宮 皇大神社|Moto-Ise Shrines=Moto-Ise Shrines|lt=Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine|12=draft}}, reflecting its connection to the {{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}} of Ise Grand Shrine. Other Moto-Ise shrines in the Ōe area are: {|class="wikitable" |- !colspan="3" | {{ill|Three Moto-Ise Shrines of Fukuchiyama|ja|元伊勢三社|12=draft}} |- |'''{{ill|Kotai Shrine|ja|皇大神社 (福知山市)|12=draft}}''' |Ōe-chō Naikū |Associated with the Inner Shrine (Naikū) of Ise Grand Shrine |- |'''{{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社|lt=Toyouke Daijinja|12=draft}}''' |Ōe-chō Tadauchi |Associated with the Outer Shrine (Gekū) of Ise Grand Shrine |- |'''{{ill|Amanoiwato_Shrine_(Fukuchiyama)|ja|天岩戸神社_(福知山市)|lt=Amanoiwato Shrine|12=draft}}''' |Ōe-chō Busshōji |Kōtai Shrine’s auxiliary shrine |} Additional shrines also claim Yoshisa-no-Miya heritage, including {{ill|Kono Shrine|ja|籠神社#境外社|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sasahara Shrine|ja|笶原神社|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Takeno Shrine|ja|竹野神社|12=draft}}. == deity == * '''{{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|12=draft}}''' - Ancestral goddess of the Imperial family and deity of Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine). == History == According to the ''{{ill|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|12=draft}}'', in the 39th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft}}, {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|12=draft}} brought Amaterasu Ōmikami to Yoshisa-no-Miya in Tanba Province for four years. Kōtai Shrine is believed to stand on this site, commemorating this temporary enshrinement. The shrine's records state that it was later reconstructed during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇|12=draft}} (707–715). Alternative traditions link the shrine to {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子|12=draft}}, who reportedly established it during his suppression of local rebels. By the Edo period, Kōtai Shrine had significant support, including land grants and periodic reconstructions by the lords of Miyazu Domain. == Grounds == File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 本殿.JPG|'''Main Hall'''
Shinmei-zukuri with thatched roof. Features inward-curving {{ill|Chigi (architecture)|ja|千木|12=draft}} and {{ill|Katsuogi|ja|鰹木|12=draft}}. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 拝殿.JPG|'''Worship Hall''' File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 入口.JPG|'''Torii'''
A blackwood torii, similar to those at {{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社|12=draft}} and {{ill|Nonomiya Shrine|ja|野宮神社|12=draft}}. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 和泉式部歌塚.JPG|'''Izumi Shikibu Monument''' The main hall is surrounded by 83 auxiliary shrines arranged in a U-shape. * Sacred Cedar of Ryūtō * Cedar planted by {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子|12=draft}} == Subsidiary Shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === Located to the left and right of the main hall: * Takuha Chichihime Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Takuha Chichihime|ja|栲幡千千姫命|1=Takuhadachiji-hime|12=draft}} * Amano-Tajikarao Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Amano-Tajikarao|ja|天手力雄命|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao|12=draft}} === Other Shrines === * 83 shrines surrounding the main hall. == Festivals == * '''Hassaku Festival''' (September 1): Includes a procession inspired by the feudal lord’s pilgrimage, reenacted with a daimyo-style parade. == Access == ; Address * Ōe-chō Naikū, Fukuchiyama City, Kyoto Prefecture 217 ; Transportation * '''By Train:''' 15-minute walk from {{ill|Ōeyamaguchi-Naikū Station|ja|大江山口内宮駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道|12=draft}}. * '''By Car:''' Accessible via Kyoto Prefecture Route 9. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{commons cat|Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)}} * [http://motoise-naiku.com/ Official Website of Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine] * [https://www.uminokyoto.jp/genryu/fukuchiyama/page4.html Moto-Ise Shrines - Fukuchiyama City - Sea of Kyoto] (Kyoto Northern Region Promotion Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:皇大神社 (福知山市)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fukuchiyama]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Fukuchiyama]] [[Category:Former Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Miyazu Domain]] [[Category:Kotai Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kōtai Shrine]] [[ja:皇大神社 (福知山市)]] [[en:Kotai Shrine]] [[ja:皇大神社]]

File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 和泉式部歌塚.JPG|'''Izumi Shikibu Monument''' The main hall is surrounded by 83 auxiliary shrines arranged in a U-shape. * Sacred Cedar of Ryūtō * Cedar planted by {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子|12=draft}} == Subsidiary Shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === Located to the left and right of the main hall: * Takuha Chichihime Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Takuha Chichihime|ja|栲幡千千姫命|1=Takuhadachiji-hime|12=draft}} * Amano-Tajikarao Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Amano-Tajikarao|ja|天手力雄命|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao|12=draft}} === Other Shrines === * 83 shrines surrounding the main hall. == Festivals == * '''Hassaku Festival''' (September 1): Includes a procession inspired by the feudal lord’s pilgrimage, reenacted with a daimyo-style parade. == Access == ; Address * Ōe-chō Naikū, Fukuchiyama City, Kyoto Prefecture 217 ; Transportation * '''By Train:''' 15-minute walk from {{ill|Ōeyamaguchi-Naikū Station|ja|大江山口内宮駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道|12=draft}}. * '''By Car:''' Accessible via Kyoto Prefecture Route 9. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{commons cat|Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)}} * [http://motoise-naiku.com/ Official Website of Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine] * [https://www.uminokyoto.jp/genryu/fukuchiyama/page4.html Moto-Ise Shrines - Fukuchiyama City - Sea of Kyoto] (Kyoto Northern Region Promotion Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:皇大神社 (福知山市)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fukuchiyama]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Fukuchiyama]] [[Category:Former Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Miyazu Domain]] [[Category:Kotai Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kōtai Shrine]] [[ja:皇大神社 (福知山市)]] [[en:Kotai Shrine]] [[ja:皇大神社]]

revision.text:
{{otheruses}} {{Shrine |name = Kōtai Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kotaijinzya shrine.jpg|280px]]
Entrance |location = {{ill|Fukuchiyama|ja|福知山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}} Ōe-chō Naikū Aza Miyama 217 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|25|48.5|N|135|09|16.2|E|region:JP-26|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=draft}} |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社|12=draft}} |Established = Traditionally, 39th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft}} |Main hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft}} (thatch roof) |Other names = Moto-Ise Kōtai Jingū, Moto-Ise Naikū |Festival date = {{ill|April 26|en|4月26日|12=draft}} |Rituals = Hassaku Parade ({{ill|September 1|en|9月1日|12=draft}}) }} '''Kōtai Shrine''' (皇大神社, '''Kōtai Jinja''') is a shrine located in the Ōe district of {{ill|Fukuchiyama|ja|福知山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Moto-Ise|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|12=draft}} legend as one of the temporary enshrinements of {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=draft}} before settling in {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft}}. == Overview == Amaterasu Ōmikami, now enshrined at {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft}}, was traditionally worshipped within the Imperial Palace. To relieve the awe of this presence, Emperor Sujin ordered the search for a suitable enshrinement location in the 6th year of his reign. After many relocations, the deity settled in Ise in the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign. The temporary enshrinement sites along this journey are remembered as the {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|12=draft}}. Kōtai Shrine is one of these Moto-Ise sites, located at the historical location known as Yoshisa-no-Miya (吉佐宮). As such, it is also referred to as {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢内宮 皇大神社|Moto-Ise Shrines=Moto-Ise Shrines|lt=Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine|12=draft}}, reflecting its connection to the {{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}} of Ise Grand Shrine. Other Moto-Ise shrines in the Ōe area are: {|class="wikitable" |- !colspan="3" | {{ill|Three Moto-Ise Shrines of Fukuchiyama|ja|元伊勢三社|12=draft}} |- |'''{{ill|Kotai Shrine|ja|皇大神社 (福知山市)|12=draft}}''' |Ōe-chō Naikū |Associated with the Inner Shrine (Naikū) of Ise Grand Shrine |- |'''{{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社|lt=Toyouke Daijinja|12=draft}}''' |Ōe-chō Tadauchi |Associated with the Outer Shrine (Gekū) of Ise Grand Shrine |- |'''{{ill|Amanoiwato_Shrine_(Fukuchiyama)|ja|天岩戸神社_(福知山市)|lt=Amanoiwato Shrine|12=draft}}''' |Ōe-chō Busshōji |Kōtai Shrine’s auxiliary shrine |} Additional shrines also claim Yoshisa-no-Miya heritage, including {{ill|Kono Shrine|ja|籠神社#境外社|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sasahara Shrine|ja|笶原神社|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Takeno Shrine|ja|竹野神社|12=draft}}. == deity == * '''{{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=draft}}''' - Ancestral goddess of the Imperial family and deity of Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine). == History == According to the ''{{ill|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|12=draft}}'', in the 39th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft}}, {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|12=draft}} brought Amaterasu Ōmikami to Yoshisa-no-Miya in Tanba Province for four years. Kōtai Shrine is believed to stand on this site, commemorating this temporary enshrinement. The shrine's records state that it was later reconstructed during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇|12=draft}} (707–715). Alternative traditions link the shrine to {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子|12=draft}}, who reportedly established it during his suppression of local rebels. By the Edo period, Kōtai Shrine had significant support, including land grants and periodic reconstructions by the lords of Miyazu Domain. == Grounds == File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 本殿.JPG|'''Main Hall'''
Shinmei-zukuri with thatched roof. Features inward-curving {{ill|Chigi (architecture)|ja|千木|12=draft}} and {{ill|Katsuogi|ja|鰹木|12=draft}}. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 拝殿.JPG|'''Worship Hall''' File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 入口.JPG|'''Torii'''
A blackwood torii, similar to those at {{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社|12=draft}} and {{ill|Nonomiya Shrine|ja|野宮神社|12=draft}}. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 和泉式部歌塚.JPG|'''Izumi Shikibu Monument''' The main hall is surrounded by 83 auxiliary shrines arranged in a U-shape. * Sacred Cedar of Ryūtō * Cedar planted by {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子|12=draft}} == Subsidiary Shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === Located to the left and right of the main hall: * Takuha Chichihime Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Takuha Chichihime|ja|栲幡千千姫命|1=Takuhadachiji-hime|12=draft}} * Amano-Tajikarao Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Amano-Tajikarao|ja|天手力雄命|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao|12=draft}} === Other Shrines === * 83 shrines surrounding the main hall. == Festivals == * '''Hassaku Festival''' (September 1): Includes a procession inspired by the feudal lord’s pilgrimage, reenacted with a daimyo-style parade. == Access == ; Address * Ōe-chō Naikū, Fukuchiyama City, Kyoto Prefecture 217 ; Transportation * '''By Train:''' 15-minute walk from {{ill|Ōeyamaguchi-Naikū Station|ja|大江山口内宮駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道|12=draft}}. * '''By Car:''' Accessible via Kyoto Prefecture Route 9. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{commons cat|Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)}} * [http://motoise-naiku.com/ Official Website of Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine] * [https://www.uminokyoto.jp/genryu/fukuchiyama/page4.html Moto-Ise Shrines - Fukuchiyama City - Sea of Kyoto] (Kyoto Northern Region Promotion Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:皇大神社 (福知山市)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fukuchiyama]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Fukuchiyama]] [[Category:Former Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Miyazu Domain]] [[Category:Kotai Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kōtai Shrine]] [[ja:皇大神社 (福知山市)]] [[en:Kotai Shrine]] [[ja:皇大神社]]

Shinmei-zukuri with thatched roof. Features inward-curving {{ill|Chigi (architecture)|ja|千木|12=draft}} and {{ill|Katsuogi|ja|鰹木|12=draft}}. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 拝殿.JPG|'''Worship Hall''' File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 入口.JPG|'''Torii'''
A blackwood torii, similar to those at {{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社|12=draft}} and {{ill|Nonomiya Shrine|ja|野宮神社|12=draft}}. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 和泉式部歌塚.JPG|'''Izumi Shikibu Monument''' The main hall is surrounded by 83 auxiliary shrines arranged in a U-shape. * Sacred Cedar of Ryūtō * Cedar planted by {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子|12=draft}} == Subsidiary Shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === Located to the left and right of the main hall: * Takuha Chichihime Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Takuha Chichihime|ja|栲幡千千姫命|1=Takuhadachiji-hime|12=draft}} * Amano-Tajikarao Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Amano-Tajikarao|ja|天手力雄命|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao|12=draft}} === Other Shrines === * 83 shrines surrounding the main hall. == Festivals == * '''Hassaku Festival''' (September 1): Includes a procession inspired by the feudal lord’s pilgrimage, reenacted with a daimyo-style parade. == Access == ; Address * Ōe-chō Naikū, Fukuchiyama City, Kyoto Prefecture 217 ; Transportation * '''By Train:''' 15-minute walk from {{ill|Ōeyamaguchi-Naikū Station|ja|大江山口内宮駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道|12=draft}}. * '''By Car:''' Accessible via Kyoto Prefecture Route 9. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{commons cat|Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)}} * [http://motoise-naiku.com/ Official Website of Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine] * [https://www.uminokyoto.jp/genryu/fukuchiyama/page4.html Moto-Ise Shrines - Fukuchiyama City - Sea of Kyoto] (Kyoto Northern Region Promotion Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:皇大神社 (福知山市)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fukuchiyama]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Fukuchiyama]] [[Category:Former Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Pages to be translated]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Miyazu Domain]] [[Category:Kotai Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kōtai Shrine]] [[ja:皇大神社 (福知山市)]] [[en:Kotai Shrine]] [[ja:皇大神社]]

revision.text:
{{otheruses}} {{Shrine |name = Kōtai Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kotaijinzya shrine.jpg|280px]]
Entrance |location = {{ill|Fukuchiyama|ja|福知山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}} Ōe-chō Naikū Aza Miyama 217 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|25|48.5|N|135|09|16.2|E|region:JP-26|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=draft}} |Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社|12=draft}} |Established = Traditionally, 39th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft}} |Main hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft}} (thatch roof) |Other names = Moto-Ise Kōtai Jingū, Moto-Ise Naikū |Festival date = {{ill|April 26|en|4月26日|12=draft}} |Rituals = Hassaku Parade ({{ill|September 1|en|9月1日|12=draft}}) }} '''Kōtai Shrine''' (皇大神社, '''Kōtai Jinja''') is a shrine located in the Ōe district of {{ill|Fukuchiyama|ja|福知山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Moto-Ise|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|12=draft}} legend as one of the temporary enshrinements of {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=draft}} before settling in {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft}}. == Overview == Amaterasu Ōmikami, now enshrined at {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft}}, was traditionally worshipped within the Imperial Palace. To relieve the awe of this presence, Emperor Sujin ordered the search for a suitable enshrinement location in the 6th year of his reign. After many relocations, the deity settled in Ise in the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign. The temporary enshrinement sites along this journey are remembered as the {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|12=draft}}. Kōtai Shrine is one of these Moto-Ise sites, located at the historical location known as Yoshisa-no-Miya (吉佐宮). As such, it is also referred to as {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢内宮 皇大神社|Moto-Ise Shrines=Moto-Ise Shrines|lt=Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine|12=draft}}, reflecting its connection to the {{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}} of Ise Grand Shrine. Other Moto-Ise shrines in the Ōe area are: {|class="wikitable" |- !colspan="3" | {{ill|Three Moto-Ise Shrines of Fukuchiyama|ja|元伊勢三社|12=draft}} |- |'''{{ill|Kotai Shrine|ja|皇大神社 (福知山市)|12=draft}}''' |Ōe-chō Naikū |Associated with the Inner Shrine (Naikū) of Ise Grand Shrine |- |'''{{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社|lt=Toyouke Daijinja|12=draft}}''' |Ōe-chō Tadauchi |Associated with the Outer Shrine (Gekū) of Ise Grand Shrine |- |'''{{ill|Amanoiwato_Shrine_(Fukuchiyama)|ja|天岩戸神社_(福知山市)|lt=Amanoiwato Shrine|12=draft}}''' |Ōe-chō Busshōji |Kōtai Shrine’s auxiliary shrine |} Additional shrines also claim Yoshisa-no-Miya heritage, including {{ill|Kono Shrine|ja|籠神社#境外社|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sasahara Shrine|ja|笶原神社|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Takeno Shrine|ja|竹野神社|12=draft}}. == deity == * '''{{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=draft}}''' - Ancestral goddess of the Imperial family and deity of Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine). == History == According to the ''{{ill|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|12=draft}}'', in the 39th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft}}, {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|12=draft}} brought Amaterasu Ōmikami to Yoshisa-no-Miya in Tanba Province for four years. Kōtai Shrine is believed to stand on this site, commemorating this temporary enshrinement. The shrine's records state that it was later reconstructed during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇|12=draft}} (707–715). Alternative traditions link the shrine to {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子|12=draft}}, who reportedly established it during his suppression of local rebels. By the Edo period, Kōtai Shrine had significant support, including land grants and periodic reconstructions by the lords of Miyazu Domain. == Grounds == File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 本殿.JPG|'''Main Hall'''
Shinmei-zukuri with thatched roof. Features inward-curving {{ill|Chigi (architecture)|ja|千木|12=draft}} and {{ill|Katsuogi|ja|鰹木|12=draft}}. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 拝殿.JPG|'''Worship Hall''' File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 入口.JPG|'''Torii'''
A blackwood torii, similar to those at {{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社|12=draft}} and {{ill|Nonomiya Shrine|ja|野宮神社|12=draft}}. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 和泉式部歌塚.JPG|'''Izumi Shikibu Monument''' The main hall is surrounded by 83 auxiliary shrines arranged in a U-shape. * Sacred Cedar of Ryūtō * Cedar planted by {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子|12=draft}} == Subsidiary Shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === Located to the left and right of the main hall: * Takuha Chichihime Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Takuha Chichihime|ja|栲幡千千姫命|1=Takuhadachiji-hime|12=draft}} * Amano-Tajikarao Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Amano-Tajikarao|ja|天手力雄命|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao|12=draft}} === Other Shrines === * 83 shrines surrounding the main hall. == Festivals == * '''Hassaku Festival''' (September 1): Includes a procession inspired by the feudal lord’s pilgrimage, reenacted with a daimyo-style parade. == Access == ; Address * Ōe-chō Naikū, Fukuchiyama City, Kyoto Prefecture 217 ; Transportation * '''By Train:''' 15-minute walk from {{ill|Ōeyamaguchi-Naikū Station|ja|大江山口内宮駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道|12=draft}}. * '''By Car:''' Accessible via Kyoto Prefecture Route 9. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{commons cat|Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)}} * [http://motoise-naiku.com/ Official Website of Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine] * [https://www.uminokyoto.jp/genryu/fukuchiyama/page4.html Moto-Ise Shrines - Fukuchiyama City - Sea of Kyoto] (Kyoto Northern Region Promotion Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:皇大神社 (福知山市)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fukuchiyama]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Fukuchiyama]] [[Category:Former Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Miyazu Domain]] [[Category:Kotai Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kōtai Shrine]] [[ja:皇大神社 (福知山市)]] [[en:Kotai Shrine]] [[ja:皇大神社]]

{{otheruses}} {{Shrine |name = Kōtai Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kotaijinzya shrine.jpg|280px]]
Entrance |location = {{ill|Fukuchiyama|ja|福知山市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}} Ōe-chō Naikū Aza Miyama 217 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|25|48.5|N|135|09|16.2|E|region:JP-26|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}} |Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社}} |Established = Traditionally, 39th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} |Main hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} (thatch roof) |Other names = Moto-Ise Kōtai Jingū, Moto-Ise Naikū |Festival date = {{ill|April 26|en|4月26日}} |Rituals = Hassaku Parade ({{ill|September 1|en|9月1日}}) }} '''Kōtai Shrine''' (皇大神社, '''Kōtai Jinja''') is a shrine located in the Ōe district of {{ill|Fukuchiyama|ja|福知山市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Moto-Ise|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines}} legend as one of the temporary enshrinements of {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}} before settling in {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. == Overview == Amaterasu Ōmikami, now enshrined at {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, was traditionally worshipped within the Imperial Palace. To relieve the awe of this presence, Emperor Sujin ordered the search for a suitable enshrinement location in the 6th year of his reign. After many relocations, the deity settled in Ise in the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign. The temporary enshrinement sites along this journey are remembered as the {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines}}. Kōtai Shrine is one of these Moto-Ise sites, located at the historical location known as Yoshisa-no-Miya (吉佐宮). Other Moto-Ise shrines in the Ōe area are: {|class="wikitable" |- !colspan="3" | {{ill|Three Moto-Ise Shrines of Fukuchiyama|ja|元伊勢三社}} |- |'''{{ill|Kotai Shrine|ja|皇大神社 (福知山市)}}''' |Ōe-chō Naikū |Associated with the Inner Shrine (Naikū) of Ise Grand Shrine |- |'''{{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社|lt=Toyouke Daijinja}}''' |Ōe-chō Tadauchi |Associated with the Outer Shrine (Gekū) of Ise Grand Shrine |- |'''{{ill|Amanoiwato_Shrine_(Fukuchiyama)|ja|天岩戸神社_(福知山市)|lt=Amanoiwato Shrine}}''' |Ōe-chō Busshōji |Kōtai Shrine’s auxiliary shrine |} Additional shrines also claim Yoshisa-no-Miya heritage, including {{ill|Kono Shrine|ja|籠神社#境外社}}, {{ill|Sasahara Shrine|ja|笶原神社}}, and {{ill|Takeno Shrine|ja|竹野神社}}. == deity == * '''{{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}''' - Ancestral goddess of the Imperial family and deity of Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine). == History == According to the ''{{ill|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki}}'', in the 39th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime}} brought Amaterasu Ōmikami to Yoshisa-no-Miya in Tanba Province for four years. Kōtai Shrine is believed to stand on this site, commemorating this temporary enshrinement. The shrine's records state that it was later reconstructed during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}} (707–715). Alternative traditions link the shrine to {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子}}, who reportedly established it during his suppression of local rebels. By the Edo period, Kōtai Shrine had significant support, including land grants and periodic reconstructions by the lords of Miyazu Domain. == Grounds == File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 本殿.JPG|'''Main Hall'''
Shinmei-zukuri with thatched roof. Features inward-curving {{ill|Chigi (architecture)|ja|千木}} and {{ill|Katsuogi|ja|鰹木}}. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 拝殿.JPG|'''Worship Hall''' File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 入口.JPG|'''Torii'''

File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 和泉式部歌塚.JPG|'''Izumi Shikibu Monument''' The main hall is surrounded by 83 auxiliary shrines arranged in a U-shape. * Sacred Cedar of Ryūtō * Cedar planted by {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子}} == Subsidiary Shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === Located to the left and right of the main hall: * Takuha Chichihime Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Takuha Chichihime|ja|栲幡千千姫命|1=Takuhadachiji-hime}} * Amano-Tajikarao Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Amano-Tajikarao|ja|天手力雄命|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}} === Other Shrines === * 83 shrines surrounding the main hall. == Festivals == * '''Hassaku Festival''' (September 1): Includes a procession inspired by the feudal lord’s pilgrimage, reenacted with a daimyo-style parade. == Access == ; Address * Ōe-chō Naikū, Fukuchiyama City, Kyoto Prefecture 217 ; Transportation * '''By Train:''' 15-minute walk from {{ill|Ōeyamaguchi-Naikū Station|ja|大江山口内宮駅}} on the {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道}}. * '''By Car:''' Accessible via Kyoto Prefecture Route 9. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{commons cat|Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)}} * [http://motoise-naiku.com/ Official Website of Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine] * [https://www.uminokyoto.jp/genryu/fukuchiyama/page4.html Moto-Ise Shrines - Fukuchiyama City - Sea of Kyoto] (Kyoto Northern Region Promotion Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:皇大神社 (福知山市)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fukuchiyama]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Fukuchiyama]] [[Category:Former Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Miyazu Domain]] [[Category:Kotai Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kōtai Shrine]] [[ja:皇大神社 (福知山市)]] [[en:Kotai Shrine]] [[ja:皇大神社]]

revision.comment: 31 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:皇大神社 (福知山市) up to rev 98851382
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 皇大神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Kotaijinzya shrine.jpg|280px]]
入口 |所在地 = [[京都府]][[福知山市]]大江町内宮字宮山217 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|25|48.5|N|135|09|16.2|E|region:JP-26|name=}} |祭神 = [[天照大神]] |社格 = 旧[[府社]] |創建 = （伝）崇神天皇39年 |本殿 = [[神明造]][[茅葺]] |別名 = 元伊勢皇大神宮・元伊勢内宮 |例祭 = [[4月26日]] |神事 = 八朔練込祭（[[9月1日]]） }} '''皇大神社'''（こうたいじんじゃ）は、[[京都府]][[福知山市]]大江町内宮にある神社。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[府社]]。[[元伊勢]]伝承地（[[吉佐宮]]）の1つ。 == 概要 == 現在[[伊勢神宮]]の祭神である[[天照大御神]]は、古くは宮中に祀られていた。この状態を畏怖した天皇の命で、[[崇神天皇]]6年から鎮座地を求めて各地を転々とした。[[垂仁天皇]]25年最終的に伊勢に落ち着くが、それまでの間に訪れた一時遷座地は、各地で'''[[元伊勢]]'''として語り継がれた。 当社はそうした元伊勢伝承地の1つで、「[[丹波国|但波（丹波）国]]乃[[吉佐宮]]」『[[倭姫命世記]]』。 の旧跡にあたる。そのため「元伊勢」を冠して称され、また特に[[皇大神宮|皇大神宮（伊勢神宮内宮）]]の元宮であるとの伝承から、'''元伊勢内宮 皇大神社'''とも称される。 市内大江地域には当社を加えて以下の元伊勢伝承の神社があり、総称して「元伊勢三社」と呼ばれている。 {|class="wikitable" |- !colspan="3" | 福知山市大江町の元伊勢三社 |- |'''皇大神社''' |福知山市大江町内宮 |[[皇大神宮|皇大神宮（伊勢神宮内宮）]]の元宮伝承地 |- |'''[[豊受大神社]]''' |福知山市大江町天田内 |[[豊受大神宮|豊受大神宮（伊勢神宮外宮）]]の元宮伝承地 |- |'''[[天岩戸神社 (福知山市)|天岩戸神社]]''' |福知山市大江町佛性寺 |皇大神社奥宮 |} なお、吉佐宮の伝承を有する神社には、当社の他にも[[籠神社#境外社|真名井神社]]（[[籠神社]]摂社）、[[笶原神社]]、[[竹野神社]]がある。 == 祭神 == * '''[[天照大神]]''' - [[皇祖神]]、伊勢の[[皇大神宮|皇大神宮（内宮）]][[祭神]] == 歴史 == 『倭姫命世記』に、[[崇神天皇]]39年、天照太神を奉じた[[豊鍬入姫命|豊鋤入姫命]]が鎮座地を求めて但波（丹波）国へ遷幸し、吉佐宮を築いて4年間奉斎したと記すが同書は続けて「此歳豊宇介神天降坐奉御饗」と、その時に豊宇介神（[[豊受大神]]）が天降って天照太神に[[神饌]]を奉った事を記し、伊勢の豊受大神宮（外宮）が[[雄略天皇|雄略天皇朝]]に丹波国から遷座したとの伝承の伏線をなしている。ちなみに大江町天田内に鎮座する豊受大神社は外宮の元宮であるとされている。、社伝によれば、当神社はその旧跡であり、天照大神が吉佐宮から遷座した後もその神徳を慕った人々が引き続き伊勢神宮内宮の元の宮として崇敬してきたといい、[[元明天皇|元明天皇朝]]（707 - 15年）に社殿を建立したという『神社名鑑』昭和38年。。また、それとは別に、[[用明天皇]]の皇子[[麻呂子親王]]が当地の兇賊を成敗するに際して[[勧請]]したものであるとの異伝もある（[[宝暦]]11年（[[1761年]]）の『丹後州宮津府志』木下幸吉編『丹後郷土史料集』第2輯〔上〕（[[名著出版]]、昭和47年）に所収。 所引『天橋記』）。 近世以前の沿革は不明であるが、[[江戸時代]]には4[[石 (単位)|石]]3[[斗]]4[[合]]の[[社領]]を有し（[[天和 (日本)|天和]]元年（[[1681年]]）の『宮津領村高帳』）[[延宝]]5年（[[1677年]]）に[[徳川家]]から寄進されたという（『神道大辞典』昭和14年）。、[[明暦]]2年（[[1656年]]）に[[宮津藩|宮津藩主]][[京極高国]]が社殿を造営し（社蔵棟札）、[[元禄]]14年（[[1701年]]）に同[[奥平昌成]]も修造してから歴代宮津藩主もこれに倣ったといい『神道大辞典』昭和14年。、また60年に1度の[[式年遷宮]]も行われていた白水社、昭和60年。。 [[昭和]]5年（[[1930年]]）府社に列し、戦後は[[神社本庁]]に参加している。 == 境内 == ファイル:皇大神社 (福知山市) 本殿.JPG|'''本殿'''
桁行3[[間]]梁間2間の[[神明造]][[茅葺]]。内削ぎの[[千木]]を持ち、棟に[[鰹木]]を置く。 ファイル:皇大神社 (福知山市) 拝殿.JPG|'''拝殿''' ファイル:皇大神社 (福知山市) 入口.JPG|'''鳥居''' 豊受大神社の鳥居と同様の[[黒木]]造りの鳥居。類似した黒木鳥居が[[野宮神社]]に存在する。 ファイル:皇大神社 (福知山市) 和泉式部歌塚.JPG|'''和泉式部歌塚''' 中心に本殿があり、本殿を囲む形でコの字形に末社83社が鎮座している。 * 龍燈の杉 * [[当麻皇子|麻呂子親王]]手植の杉 == 摂末社 == === 摂社（脇宮） === 本殿の両脇に鎮座。 * 栲機千々姫社 - 本殿左。祭神：[[栲幡千千姫命|栲幡千々姫命]] * 天手力雄命社 - 本殿右。祭神：[[天手力雄命]] === 末社 === * 計83社[[ファイル:皇大神社.png|サムネイル|皇大神社の社殿配置図]] == 主な祭事 == * [[9月1日]] 八朔祭 :: かつて宮津藩主が祈願のため行列をなして参拝したといい、それに因んで大名行列を模して練り歩く神事（練込）が行われる。古くは「笹囃子」もあったという。 == 現地情報 == ; 所在地 * [[京都府]][[福知山市]]大江町内宮字宮山217 ; 交通アクセス {{underline|鉄道}} * 最寄駅：[[京都丹後鉄道]] [[京都丹後鉄道宮福線|宮福線]] [[大江山口内宮駅]] （徒歩15分） {{underline|車}} * [[京都府道9号綾部大江宮津線]] ; 周辺 * [[天岩戸神社 (福知山市)|天岩戸神社]] - 当社の奥宮 * 五十鈴川 - 当社近くを流れる。伊勢神宮（内宮）前の[[五十鈴川]]と同名 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * 神社本庁調査部編『神社名鑑』（神社本庁、昭和38年） * [[谷川健一]]編『日本の神々－神社と聖地』第7巻山陰《新装復刊》（[[白水社]]、平成12年 ISBN 978-4-560-02507-9（初版は昭和60年 ISBN 4-560-02217-8）） * [[宮地直一]]・[[佐伯有義]]監修『神道大辞典 縮刷版』（[[臨川書店]]、昭和44年 ISBN 4-653-01347-0（初版[[平凡社]]、昭和14年）） == 外部リンク == {{commons cat|Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)}} * [http://motoise-naiku.com/　元伊勢内宮 皇大神社｜公式サイト] * [https://www.uminokyoto.jp/genryu/fukuchiyama/page4.html 元伊勢三社 - 福知山市 - 海の京都]（京都府北部地域連携都市圏振興社） {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こうたいしんしやふくちやまし}} [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:福知山市の歴史]] [[Category:福知山市の建築物]] [[Category:京都府の旧府社]] [[Category:元伊勢]] [[Category:神明神社]] [[Category:宮津藩]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[皇大神社 (福知山市)]] to [[Kōtai Shrine]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

{{otheruses}} {{Shrine |name = Kōtai Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kotaijinzya shrine.jpg|280px]]
Entrance |location = {{ill|Fukuchiyama|ja|福知山市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}} Ōe-chō Naikū Aza Miyama 217 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|25|48.5|N|135|09|16.2|E|region:JP-26|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}} |Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社}} |Established = Traditionally, 39th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} |Main hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} (thatch roof) |Other names = Moto-Ise Kōtai Jingū, Moto-Ise Naikū |Festival date = {{ill|April 26|en|4月26日}} |Rituals = Hassaku Parade ({{ill|September 1|en|9月1日}}) }} '''Kōtai Shrine''' (皇大神社, '''Kōtai Jinja''') is a shrine located in the Ōe district of {{ill|Fukuchiyama|ja|福知山市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Moto-Ise|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines}} legend as one of the temporary enshrinements of {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}} before settling in {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. == Overview == Amaterasu Ōmikami, now enshrined at {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, was traditionally worshipped within the Imperial Palace. To relieve the awe of this presence, Emperor Sujin ordered the search for a suitable enshrinement location in the 6th year of his reign. After many relocations, the deity settled in Ise in the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign. The temporary enshrinement sites along this journey are remembered as the {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines}}. Kōtai Shrine is one of these Moto-Ise sites, located at the historical location known as Yoshisa-no-Miya (吉佐宮). As such, it is also referred to as {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢内宮 皇大神社|Moto-Ise Shrines=Moto-Ise Shrines|lt=Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine}}, reflecting its connection to the {{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} of Ise Grand Shrine. Other Moto-Ise shrines in the Ōe area are: {|class="wikitable" |- !colspan="3" | {{ill|Three Moto-Ise Shrines of Fukuchiyama|ja|元伊勢三社}} |- |'''{{ill|Kotai Shrine|ja|皇大神社 (福知山市)}}''' |Ōe-chō Naikū |Associated with the Inner Shrine (Naikū) of Ise Grand Shrine |- |'''{{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社|lt=Toyouke Daijinja}}''' |Ōe-chō Tadauchi |Associated with the Outer Shrine (Gekū) of Ise Grand Shrine |- |'''{{ill|Amanoiwato_Shrine_(Fukuchiyama)|ja|天岩戸神社_(福知山市)|lt=Amanoiwato Shrine}}''' |Ōe-chō Busshōji |Kōtai Shrine’s auxiliary shrine |} Additional shrines also claim Yoshisa-no-Miya heritage, including {{ill|Kono Shrine|ja|籠神社#境外社}}, {{ill|Sasahara Shrine|ja|笶原神社}}, and {{ill|Takeno Shrine|ja|竹野神社}}. == deity == * '''{{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}''' - Ancestral goddess of the Imperial family and deity of Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine). == History == According to the ''{{ill|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki}}'', in the 39th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime}} brought Amaterasu Ōmikami to Yoshisa-no-Miya in Tanba Province for four years. Kōtai Shrine is believed to stand on this site, commemorating this temporary enshrinement. The shrine's records state that it was later reconstructed during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}} (707–715). Alternative traditions link the shrine to {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子}}, who reportedly established it during his suppression of local rebels. By the Edo period, Kōtai Shrine had significant support, including land grants and periodic reconstructions by the lords of Miyazu Domain. == Grounds == File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 本殿.JPG|'''Main Hall'''
Shinmei-zukuri with thatched roof. Features inward-curving {{ill|Chigi (architecture)|ja|千木}} and {{ill|Katsuogi|ja|鰹木}}. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 拝殿.JPG|'''Worship Hall''' File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 入口.JPG|'''Torii'''
A blackwood torii, similar to those at {{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社}} and {{ill|Nonomiya Shrine|ja|野宮神社}}. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 和泉式部歌塚.JPG|'''Izumi Shikibu Monument''' The main hall is surrounded by 83 auxiliary shrines arranged in a U-shape. * Sacred Cedar of Ryūtō * Cedar planted by {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子}} == Subsidiary Shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === Located to the left and right of the main hall: * Takuha Chichihime Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Takuha Chichihime|ja|栲幡千千姫命|1=Takuhadachiji-hime}} * Amano-Tajikarao Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Amano-Tajikarao|ja|天手力雄命|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}} === Other Shrines === * 83 shrines surrounding the main hall. == Festivals == * '''Hassaku Festival''' (September 1): Includes a procession inspired by the feudal lord’s pilgrimage, reenacted with a daimyo-style parade. == Access == ; Address * Ōe-chō Naikū, Fukuchiyama City, Kyoto Prefecture 217 ; Transportation * '''By Train:''' 15-minute walk from {{ill|Ōeyamaguchi-Naikū Station|ja|大江山口内宮駅}} on the {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道}}. * '''By Car:''' Accessible via Kyoto Prefecture Route 9. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{commons cat|Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)}} * [http://motoise-naiku.com/ Official Website of Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine] * [https://www.uminokyoto.jp/genryu/fukuchiyama/page4.html Moto-Ise Shrines - Fukuchiyama City - Sea of Kyoto] (Kyoto Northern Region Promotion Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:皇大神社 (福知山市)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fukuchiyama]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Fukuchiyama]] [[Category:Former Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Miyazu Domain]] [[Category:Kotai Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kōtai Shrine]] [[ja:皇大神社 (福知山市)]] [[en:Kotai Shrine]] [[ja:皇大神社]] {{translated page|ja|皇大神社 (福知山市)|version=98851382|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Kōtai Shrine is one of these Moto-Ise sites, located at the historical location known as Yoshisa-no-Miya (吉佐宮). As such, it is also referred to as {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢内宮 皇大神社|Moto-Ise Shrines=Moto-Ise Shrines|lt=Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine|qq=draft}}, reflecting its connection to the {{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft}} of Ise Grand Shrine. Other Moto-Ise shrines in the Ōe area are: {|class="wikitable" |- !colspan="3" | {{ill|Three Moto-Ise Shrines of Fukuchiyama|ja|元伊勢三社|qq=draft}} |- |'''{{ill|Kotai Shrine|ja|皇大神社 (福知山市)|qq=draft}}''' |Ōe-chō Naikū |Associated with the Inner Shrine (Naikū) of Ise Grand Shrine |- |'''{{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社|lt=Toyouke Daijinja|qq=draft}}''' |Ōe-chō Tadauchi |Associated with the Outer Shrine (Gekū) of Ise Grand Shrine |- |'''{{ill|Amanoiwato_Shrine_(Fukuchiyama)|ja|天岩戸神社_(福知山市)|lt=Amanoiwato Shrine|qq=draft}}''' |Ōe-chō Busshōji |Kōtai Shrine’s auxiliary shrine |} Additional shrines also claim Yoshisa-no-Miya heritage, including {{ill|Kono Shrine|ja|籠神社#境外社|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sasahara Shrine|ja|笶原神社|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Takeno Shrine|ja|竹野神社|qq=draft}}. == deity == * '''{{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft}}''' - Ancestral goddess of the Imperial family and deity of Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine). == History == According to the ''{{ill|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=draft}}'', in the 39th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=draft}} brought Amaterasu Ōmikami to Yoshisa-no-Miya in Tanba Province for four years. Kōtai Shrine is believed to stand on this site, commemorating this temporary enshrinement. The shrine's records state that it was later reconstructed during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇|qq=draft}} (707–715). Alternative traditions link the shrine to {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子|qq=draft}}, who reportedly established it during his suppression of local rebels. By the Edo period, Kōtai Shrine had significant support, including land grants and periodic reconstructions by the lords of Miyazu Domain. == Grounds == File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 本殿.JPG|'''Main Hall'''
Shinmei-zukuri with thatched roof. Features inward-curving {{ill|Chigi (architecture)|ja|千木|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Katsuogi|ja|鰹木|qq=draft}}. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 拝殿.JPG|'''Worship Hall''' File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 入口.JPG|'''Torii'''
A blackwood torii, similar to those at {{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Nonomiya Shrine|ja|野宮神社|qq=draft}}. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 和泉式部歌塚.JPG|'''Izumi Shikibu Monument''' The main hall is surrounded by 83 auxiliary shrines arranged in a U-shape. * Sacred Cedar of Ryūtō * Cedar planted by {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子|qq=draft}} == Subsidiary Shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === Located to the left and right of the main hall: * Takuha Chichihime Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Takuha Chichihime|ja|栲幡千千姫命|1=Takuhadachiji-hime|qq=draft}} * Amano-Tajikarao Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Amano-Tajikarao|ja|天手力雄命|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao|qq=draft}} === Other Shrines === * 83 shrines surrounding the main hall. == Festivals == * '''Hassaku Festival''' (September 1): Includes a procession inspired by the feudal lord’s pilgrimage, reenacted with a daimyo-style parade. == Access == ; Address * Ōe-chō Naikū, Fukuchiyama City, Kyoto Prefecture 217 ; Transportation * '''By Train:''' 15-minute walk from {{ill|Ōeyamaguchi-Naikū Station|ja|大江山口内宮駅|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道|qq=draft}}. * '''By Car:''' Accessible via Kyoto Prefecture Route 9. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{commons cat|Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)}} * [http://motoise-naiku.com/ Official Website of Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine] * [https://www.uminokyoto.jp/genryu/fukuchiyama/page4.html Moto-Ise Shrines - Fukuchiyama City - Sea of Kyoto] (Kyoto Northern Region Promotion Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|皇大神社 (福知山市)|version=98851382|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:皇大神社 (福知山市)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fukuchiyama]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Fukuchiyama]] [[Category:Former Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Miyazu Domain]] [[Category:Kotai Shrine]] }} [[sn:Kōtai Shrine]] [[ja:皇大神社 (福知山市)]] [[en:Kotai Shrine]] [[ja:皇大神社]]

Entrance |location = {{ill|Fukuchiyama|ja|福知山市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft}} Ōe-chō Naikū Aza Miyama 217 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|25|48.5|N|135|09|16.2|E|region:JP-26|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft}} |Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社|qq=draft}} |Established = Traditionally, 39th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft}} |Main hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft}} (thatch roof) |Other names = Moto-Ise Kōtai Jingū, Moto-Ise Naikū |Festival date = {{ill|April 26|en|4月26日|qq=draft}} |Rituals = Hassaku Parade ({{ill|September 1|en|9月1日|qq=draft}}) }} '''Kōtai Shrine''' (皇大神社, '''Kōtai Jinja''') is a shrine located in the Ōe district of {{ill|Fukuchiyama|ja|福知山市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Moto-Ise|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=draft}} legend as one of the temporary enshrinements of {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft}} before settling in {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft}}. == Overview == Amaterasu Ōmikami, now enshrined at {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft}}, was traditionally worshipped within the Imperial Palace. To relieve the awe of this presence, Emperor Sujin ordered the search for a suitable enshrinement location in the 6th year of his reign. After many relocations, the deity settled in Ise in the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign. The temporary enshrinement sites along this journey are remembered as the {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=draft}}. Kōtai Shrine is one of these Moto-Ise sites, located at the historical location known as Yoshisa-no-Miya (吉佐宮). As such, it is also referred to as {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢内宮 皇大神社|Moto-Ise Shrines=Moto-Ise Shrines|lt=Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine|qq=draft}}, reflecting its connection to the {{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft}} of Ise Grand Shrine. Kōtai Shrine is believed to stand on this site, commemorating this temporary enshrinement. The shrine's records state that it was later reconstructed during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇|qq=draft}} (707–715). Alternative traditions link the shrine to {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子|qq=draft}}, who reportedly established it during his suppression of local rebels. By the Edo period, Kōtai Shrine had significant support, including land grants and periodic reconstructions by the lords of Miyazu Domain. == Grounds == File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 本殿.JPG|'''Main Hall'''
Shinmei-zukuri with thatched roof. Features inward-curving {{ill|Chigi (architecture)|ja|千木|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Katsuogi|ja|鰹木|qq=draft}}. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 拝殿.JPG|'''Worship Hall''' File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 入口.JPG|'''Torii'''
A blackwood torii, similar to those at {{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Nonomiya Shrine|ja|野宮神社|qq=draft}}. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 和泉式部歌塚.JPG|'''Izumi Shikibu Monument''' The main hall is surrounded by 83 auxiliary shrines arranged in a U-shape. * Sacred Cedar of Ryūtō * Cedar planted by {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子|qq=draft}} == Subsidiary Shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === Located to the left and right of the main hall: * Takuha Chichihime Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Takuha Chichihime|ja|栲幡千千姫命|1=Takuhadachiji-hime|qq=draft}} * Amano-Tajikarao Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Amano-Tajikarao|ja|天手力雄命|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao|qq=draft}} === Other Shrines === * 83 shrines surrounding the main hall. == Festivals == * '''Hassaku Festival''' (September 1): Includes a procession inspired by the feudal lord’s pilgrimage, reenacted with a daimyo-style parade. == Access == ; Address * Ōe-chō Naikū, Fukuchiyama City, Kyoto Prefecture 217 ; Transportation * '''By Train:''' 15-minute walk from {{ill|Ōeyamaguchi-Naikū Station|ja|大江山口内宮駅|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道|qq=draft}}. * '''By Car:''' Accessible via Kyoto Prefecture Route 9. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{commons cat|Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)}} * [http://motoise-naiku.com/ Official Website of Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine] * [https://www.uminokyoto.jp/genryu/fukuchiyama/page4.html Moto-Ise Shrines - Fukuchiyama City - Sea of Kyoto] (Kyoto Northern Region Promotion Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|皇大神社 (福知山市)|version=98851382|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:皇大神社 (福知山市)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fukuchiyama]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Fukuchiyama]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Miyazu Domain]] [[Category:Kotai Shrine]] }} [[sn:Kōtai Shrine]] [[ja:皇大神社 (福知山市)]] [[en:Kotai Shrine]] [[ja:皇大神社]]

Entrance |location = {{ill|Fukuchiyama|ja|福知山市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuchiyama|qq=}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Prefecture|qq=}} Ōe-chō Naikū Aza Miyama 217 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|25|48.5|N|135|09|16.2|E|region:JP-26|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}} |Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=}} |Established = Traditionally, 39th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sujin|qq=}} |Main hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei-zukuri|qq=}} (thatch roof) |Other names = Moto-Ise Kōtai Jingū, Moto-Ise Naikū |Festival date = {{ill|April 26|en|4月26日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 26|qq=}} |Rituals = Hassaku Parade ({{ill|September 1|en|9月1日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 1|qq=}}) }} '''Kōtai Shrine''' (皇大神社, '''Kōtai Jinja''') is a shrine located in the Ōe district of {{ill|Fukuchiyama|ja|福知山市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuchiyama|qq=}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Moto-Ise|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} legend as one of the temporary enshrinements of {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}} before settling in {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|qq=}}. == Overview == Amaterasu Ōmikami, now enshrined at {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|qq=}}, was traditionally worshipped within the Imperial Palace. To relieve the awe of this presence, Emperor Sujin ordered the search for a suitable enshrinement location in the 6th year of his reign. After many relocations, the deity settled in Ise in the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign. The temporary enshrinement sites along this journey are remembered as the {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}}. Kōtai Shrine is one of these Moto-Ise sites, located at the historical location known as Yoshisa-no-Miya (吉佐宮). As such, it is also referred to as {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢内宮 皇大神社|Moto-Ise Shrines=Moto-Ise Shrines|lt=Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}}, reflecting its connection to the {{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} of Ise Grand Shrine. Other Moto-Ise shrines in the Ōe area are: {|class="wikitable" |- !colspan="3" | {{ill|Three Moto-Ise Shrines of Fukuchiyama|ja|元伊勢三社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Moto-Ise Shrines of Fukuchiyama|qq=}} |- |'''{{ill|Kotai Shrine|ja|皇大神社 (福知山市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotai Shrine|qq=}}''' |Ōe-chō Naikū |Associated with the Inner Shrine (Naikū) of Ise Grand Shrine |- |'''{{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社|lt=Toyouke Daijinja|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|qq=}}''' |Ōe-chō Tadauchi |Associated with the Outer Shrine (Gekū) of Ise Grand Shrine |- |'''{{ill|Amanoiwato_Shrine_(Fukuchiyama)|ja|天岩戸神社_(福知山市)|lt=Amanoiwato Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanoiwato_Shrine_(Fukuchiyama)|qq=}}''' |Ōe-chō Busshōji |Kōtai Shrine’s auxiliary shrine |} Additional shrines also claim Yoshisa-no-Miya heritage, including {{ill|Kono Shrine|ja|籠神社#境外社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kono Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Sasahara Shrine|ja|笶原神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasahara Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Takeno Shrine|ja|竹野神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeno Shrine|qq=}}. == deity == * '''{{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}''' - Ancestral goddess of the Imperial family and deity of Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine). == History == According to the ''{{ill|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=}}'', in the 39th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sujin|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}} brought Amaterasu Ōmikami to Yoshisa-no-Miya in Tanba Province for four years. Kōtai Shrine is believed to stand on this site, commemorating this temporary enshrinement. The shrine's records state that it was later reconstructed during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Genmei|qq=}} (707–715). Alternative traditions link the shrine to {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Maroko|qq=}}, who reportedly established it during his suppression of local rebels. By the Edo period, Kōtai Shrine had significant support, including land grants and periodic reconstructions by the lords of Miyazu Domain. == Grounds == File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 本殿.JPG|'''Main Hall'''
Shinmei-zukuri with thatched roof. Features inward-curving {{ill|Chigi (architecture)|ja|千木|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigi (architecture)|qq=}} and {{ill|Katsuogi|ja|鰹木|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuogi|qq=}}. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 拝殿.JPG|'''Worship Hall''' File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 入口.JPG|'''Torii'''
A blackwood torii, similar to those at {{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|qq=}} and {{ill|Nonomiya Shrine|ja|野宮神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nonomiya Shrine|qq=}}. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 和泉式部歌塚.JPG|'''Izumi Shikibu Monument''' The main hall is surrounded by 83 auxiliary shrines arranged in a U-shape. * Sacred Cedar of Ryūtō * Cedar planted by {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Maroko|qq=}} == Subsidiary Shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === Located to the left and right of the main hall: * Takuha Chichihime Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Takuha Chichihime|ja|栲幡千千姫命|1=Takuhadachiji-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takuha Chichihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Takuhadachiji-hime|qq=}} * Amano-Tajikarao Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Amano-Tajikarao|ja|天手力雄命|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amano-Tajikarao|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tajikarao|qq=}} === Other Shrines === * 83 shrines surrounding the main hall. == Festivals == * '''Hassaku Festival''' (September 1): Includes a procession inspired by the feudal lord’s pilgrimage, reenacted with a daimyo-style parade. == Access == ; Address * Ōe-chō Naikū, Fukuchiyama City, Kyoto Prefecture 217 ; Transportation * '''By Train:''' 15-minute walk from {{ill|Ōeyamaguchi-Naikū Station|ja|大江山口内宮駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōeyamaguchi-Naikū Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Tango Railway|qq=}}. * '''By Car:''' Accessible via Kyoto Prefecture Route 9. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{commons cat|Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)}} * [http://motoise-naiku.com/ Official Website of Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine] * [https://www.uminokyoto.jp/genryu/fukuchiyama/page4.html Moto-Ise Shrines - Fukuchiyama City - Sea of Kyoto] (Kyoto Northern Region Promotion Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|皇大神社 (福知山市)|version=98851382|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:皇大神社 (福知山市)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fukuchiyama]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Fukuchiyama]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Miyazu Domain]] [[Category:Kotai Shrine]] }} [[sn:Kōtai Shrine]] [[ja:皇大神社 (福知山市)]] [[en:Kotai Shrine]] [[ja:皇大神社]]
