{{otheruses}} {{Shrine |name = Kōtai Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kotaijinzya shrine.jpg|280px]]
Entrance |location = [[:en:Fukuchiyama, Kyoto|Fukuchiyama]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]] Ōe-chō Naikū Aza Miyama 217 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|25|48.5|N|135|09|16.2|E|region:JP-26|display=inline,title}} |deity = [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] |Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|府社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |Established = Traditionally, 39th year of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] |Main hall = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] (thatch roof) |Other names = Moto-Ise Kōtai Jingū, Moto-Ise Naikū |Festival date = {{ill|April 26|en|4月26日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 26|qq=}} |Rituals = Hassaku Parade ({{ill|September 1|en|9月1日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 1|qq=}}) }} '''Kōtai Shrine''' (皇大神社, '''Kōtai Jinja''') is a shrine located in the Ōe district of [[:en:Fukuchiyama, Kyoto|Fukuchiyama]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Moto-Ise|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} legend as one of the temporary enshrinements of [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] before settling in [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. == Overview == Amaterasu Ōmikami, now enshrined at [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]], was traditionally worshipped within the Imperial Palace. To relieve the awe of this presence, Emperor Sujin ordered the search for a suitable enshrinement location in the 6th year of his reign. After many relocations, the deity settled in Ise in the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign. The temporary enshrinement sites along this journey are remembered as the {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}}. Kōtai Shrine is one of these Moto-Ise sites, located at the historical location known as Yoshisa-no-Miya (吉佐宮). As such, it is also referred to as {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢内宮 皇大神社|Moto-Ise Shrines=Moto-Ise Shrines|lt=Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, reflecting its connection to the {{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of Ise Grand Shrine. Other Moto-Ise shrines in the Ōe area are: {|class="wikitable" |- !colspan="3" | {{ill|Three Moto-Ise Shrines of Fukuchiyama|ja|元伊勢三社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Moto-Ise Shrines of Fukuchiyama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- |'''{{ill|Kōtai Shrine|lt=Kōtai Shrine|lt=Kotai Shrine|ja|皇大神社 (福知山市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotai Shrine|qq=}}''' |Ōe-chō Naikū |Associated with the Inner Shrine (Naikū) of Ise Grand Shrine |- |'''{{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社|lt=Toyouke Daijinja|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|qq=}}''' |Ōe-chō Tadauchi |Associated with the Outer Shrine (Gekū) of Ise Grand Shrine |- |'''{{ill|Amanoiwato_Shrine_(Fukuchiyama)|ja|天岩戸神社_(福知山市)|lt=Amanoiwato Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanoiwato_Shrine_(Fukuchiyama)|qq=}}''' |Ōe-chō Busshōji |Kōtai Shrine’s auxiliary shrine |} Additional shrines also claim Yoshisa-no-Miya heritage, including {{ill|Kono Shrine|ja|籠神社#境外社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kono Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Sasahara Shrine|ja|笶原神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasahara Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Takeno Shrine|ja|竹野神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeno Shrine|qq=}}. == deity == * '''[[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]''' - Ancestral goddess of the Imperial family and deity of Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine). == History == According to the ''{{ill|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=}}'', in the 39th year of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}} brought Amaterasu Ōmikami to Yoshisa-no-Miya in Tanba Province for four years. Kōtai Shrine is believed to stand on this site, commemorating this temporary enshrinement. The shrine's records state that it was later reconstructed during the reign of [[:en:Empress Genmei|Empress Genmei]] (707–715). Alternative traditions link the shrine to {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Maroko|qq=}}, who reportedly established it during his suppression of local rebels. By the Edo period, Kōtai Shrine had significant support, including land grants and periodic reconstructions by the lords of Miyazu Domain. == Grounds == File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 本殿.JPG|'''Main Hall'''
Shinmei-zukuri with thatched roof. Features inward-curving {{ill|Chigi (architecture)|ja|千木|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigi (architecture)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 千木・鰹木}} and {{ill|Katsuogi|ja|鰹木|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuogi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 千木・鰹木}}. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 拝殿.JPG|'''Worship Hall''' File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 入口.JPG|'''Torii'''
A blackwood torii, similar to those at {{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|qq=}} and [[:en:Nonomiya Shrine|Nonomiya Shrine]]. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 和泉式部歌塚.JPG|'''Izumi Shikibu Monument''' The main hall is surrounded by 83 auxiliary shrines arranged in a U-shape. * Sacred Cedar of Ryūtō * Cedar planted by {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Maroko|qq=}} == Subsidiary Shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === Located to the left and right of the main hall: * Takuha Chichihime Shrine - Dedicated to [[:en:Takuhadachiji-hime|Takuhadachiji-hime]] * Amano-Tajikarao Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Amano-Tajikarao|ja|天手力雄命|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amano-Tajikarao|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tajikarao|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノタヂカラオ}} === Other Shrines === * 83 shrines surrounding the main hall. == Festivals == * '''Hassaku Festival''' (September 1): Includes a procession inspired by the feudal lord’s pilgrimage, reenacted with a daimyo-style parade. == Access == ; Address * Ōe-chō Naikū, Fukuchiyama City, Kyoto Prefecture 217 ; Transportation * '''By Train:''' 15-minute walk from [[:en:Ōeyamaguchi-Naiku Station|Ōeyamaguchi-Naikū Station]] on the {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Tango Railway|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to WILLER TRAINS}}. * '''By Car:''' Accessible via Kyoto Prefecture Route 9. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{commons cat|Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)}} * [http://motoise-naiku.com/ Official Website of Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine] * [https://www.uminokyoto.jp/genryu/fukuchiyama/page4.html Moto-Ise Shrines - Fukuchiyama City - Sea of Kyoto] (Kyoto Northern Region Promotion Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|皇大神社 (福知山市)|version=98851382|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:皇大神社 (福知山市)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fukuchiyama]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Miyazu Domain]] }} [[sn:Kōtai Shrine]] [[ja:皇大神社 (福知山市)]] [[ja:皇大神社]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:Miyazu Domain]] [[Category:Buildings in Fukuchiyama, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Fukuchiyama, Kyoto]] {{Commons category|Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)}}

A blackwood torii, similar to those at {{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|qq=}} and [[:en:Nonomiya Shrine|Nonomiya Shrine]]. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 和泉式部歌塚.JPG|'''Izumi Shikibu Monument''' The main hall is surrounded by 83 auxiliary shrines arranged in a U-shape. * Sacred Cedar of Ryūtō * Cedar planted by {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Maroko|qq=}} == Subsidiary Shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === Located to the left and right of the main hall: * Takuha Chichihime Shrine - Dedicated to [[:en:Takuhadachiji-hime|Takuhadachiji-hime]] * Amano-Tajikarao Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Amano-Tajikarao|ja|天手力雄命|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amano-Tajikarao|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tajikarao|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノタヂカラオ}} === Other Shrines === * 83 shrines surrounding the main hall. == Festivals == * '''Hassaku Festival''' (September 1): Includes a procession inspired by the feudal lord’s pilgrimage, reenacted with a daimyo-style parade. == Access == ; Address * Ōe-chō Naikū, Fukuchiyama City, Kyoto Prefecture 217 ; Transportation * '''By Train:''' 15-minute walk from [[:en:Ōeyamaguchi-Naiku Station|Ōeyamaguchi-Naikū Station]] on the {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Tango Railway|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to WILLER TRAINS}}. * '''By Car:''' Accessible via Kyoto Prefecture Route 9. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{commons cat|Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)}} * [http://motoise-naiku.com/ Official Website of Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine] * [https://www.uminokyoto.jp/genryu/fukuchiyama/page4.html Moto-Ise Shrines - Fukuchiyama City - Sea of Kyoto] (Kyoto Northern Region Promotion Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|皇大神社 (福知山市)|version=98851382|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:皇大神社 (福知山市)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Miyazu Domain]] }} [[sn:Kōtai Shrine]] [[ja:皇大神社 (福知山市)]] [[ja:皇大神社]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:Miyazu Domain]] [[Category:Buildings in Fukuchiyama, Kyoto]] {{Commons category|Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)}}

revision.text:
{{otheruses}} {{Shrine |name = Kōtai Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kotaijinzya shrine.jpg|280px]]
Entrance |location = [[:en:Fukuchiyama, Kyoto|Fukuchiyama]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]] Ōe-chō Naikū Aza Miyama 217 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|25|48.5|N|135|09|16.2|E|region:JP-26|display=inline,title}} |deity = [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] |Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|府社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |Established = Traditionally, 39th year of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] |Main hall = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] (thatch roof) |Other names = Moto-Ise Kōtai Jingū, Moto-Ise Naikū |Festival date = {{ill|April 26|en|4月26日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 26|qq=}} |Rituals = Hassaku Parade ({{ill|September 1|en|9月1日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 1|qq=}}) }} '''Kōtai Shrine''' (皇大神社, '''Kōtai Jinja''') is a shrine located in the Ōe district of [[:en:Fukuchiyama, Kyoto|Fukuchiyama]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Moto-Ise|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} legend as one of the temporary enshrinements of [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] before settling in [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. == Overview == Amaterasu Ōmikami, now enshrined at [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]], was traditionally worshipped within the Imperial Palace. To relieve the awe of this presence, Emperor Sujin ordered the search for a suitable enshrinement location in the 6th year of his reign. After many relocations, the deity settled in Ise in the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign. The temporary enshrinement sites along this journey are remembered as the {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}}. Kōtai Shrine is one of these Moto-Ise sites, located at the historical location known as Yoshisa-no-Miya (吉佐宮). As such, it is also referred to as {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢内宮 皇大神社|Moto-Ise Shrines=Moto-Ise Shrines|lt=Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, reflecting its connection to the {{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of Ise Grand Shrine. Other Moto-Ise shrines in the Ōe area are: {|class="wikitable" |- !colspan="3" | {{ill|Three Moto-Ise Shrines of Fukuchiyama|ja|元伊勢三社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Moto-Ise Shrines of Fukuchiyama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- |'''{{ill|Kōtai Shrine|lt=Kōtai Shrine|lt=Kotai Shrine|ja|皇大神社 (福知山市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotai Shrine|qq=}}''' |Ōe-chō Naikū |Associated with the Inner Shrine (Naikū) of Ise Grand Shrine |- |'''{{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社|lt=Toyouke Daijinja|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|qq=}}''' |Ōe-chō Tadauchi |Associated with the Outer Shrine (Gekū) of Ise Grand Shrine |- |'''{{ill|Amanoiwato_Shrine_(Fukuchiyama)|ja|天岩戸神社_(福知山市)|lt=Amanoiwato Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanoiwato_Shrine_(Fukuchiyama)|qq=}}''' |Ōe-chō Busshōji |Kōtai Shrine’s auxiliary shrine |} Additional shrines also claim Yoshisa-no-Miya heritage, including {{ill|Kono Shrine|ja|籠神社#境外社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kono Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Sasahara Shrine|ja|笶原神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasahara Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Takeno Shrine|ja|竹野神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeno Shrine|qq=}}. == deity == * '''[[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]''' - Ancestral goddess of the Imperial family and deity of Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine). == History == According to the ''{{ill|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=}}'', in the 39th year of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}} brought Amaterasu Ōmikami to Yoshisa-no-Miya in Tanba Province for four years. Kōtai Shrine is believed to stand on this site, commemorating this temporary enshrinement. The shrine's records state that it was later reconstructed during the reign of [[:en:Empress Genmei|Empress Genmei]] (707–715). Alternative traditions link the shrine to {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Maroko|qq=}}, who reportedly established it during his suppression of local rebels. By the Edo period, Kōtai Shrine had significant support, including land grants and periodic reconstructions by the lords of Miyazu Domain. == Grounds == File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 本殿.JPG|'''Main Hall'''
Shinmei-zukuri with thatched roof. Features inward-curving {{ill|Chigi (architecture)|ja|千木|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigi (architecture)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 千木・鰹木}} and {{ill|Katsuogi|ja|鰹木|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuogi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 千木・鰹木}}. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 拝殿.JPG|'''Worship Hall''' File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 入口.JPG|'''Torii'''
A blackwood torii, similar to those at {{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|qq=}} and [[:en:Nonomiya Shrine|Nonomiya Shrine]]. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 和泉式部歌塚.JPG|'''Izumi Shikibu Monument''' The main hall is surrounded by 83 auxiliary shrines arranged in a U-shape. * Sacred Cedar of Ryūtō * Cedar planted by {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Maroko|qq=}} == Subsidiary Shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === Located to the left and right of the main hall: * Takuha Chichihime Shrine - Dedicated to [[:en:Takuhadachiji-hime|Takuhadachiji-hime]] * Amano-Tajikarao Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Amano-Tajikarao|ja|天手力雄命|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amano-Tajikarao|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tajikarao|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノタヂカラオ}} === Other Shrines === * 83 shrines surrounding the main hall. == Festivals == * '''Hassaku Festival''' (September 1): Includes a procession inspired by the feudal lord’s pilgrimage, reenacted with a daimyo-style parade. == Access == ; Address * Ōe-chō Naikū, Fukuchiyama City, Kyoto Prefecture 217 ; Transportation * '''By Train:''' 15-minute walk from [[:en:Ōeyamaguchi-Naiku Station|Ōeyamaguchi-Naikū Station]] on the {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Tango Railway|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to WILLER TRAINS}}. * '''By Car:''' Accessible via Kyoto Prefecture Route 9. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{commons cat|Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)}} * [http://motoise-naiku.com/ Official Website of Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine] * [https://www.uminokyoto.jp/genryu/fukuchiyama/page4.html Moto-Ise Shrines - Fukuchiyama City - Sea of Kyoto] (Kyoto Northern Region Promotion Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|皇大神社 (福知山市)|version=98851382|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Miyazu Domain]] }} [[sn:Kōtai Shrine]] [[ja:皇大神社 (福知山市)]] [[ja:皇大神社]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:Miyazu Domain]] [[Category:Buildings in Fukuchiyama, Kyoto]] {{Commons category|Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)}}

{{otheruses}} {{Shrine |name = Kōtai Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kotaijinzya shrine.jpg|280px]]
Entrance |location = [[:en:Fukuchiyama, Kyoto|Fukuchiyama]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]] Ōe-chō Naikū Aza Miyama 217 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|25|48.5|N|135|09|16.2|E|region:JP-26|display=inline,title}} |deity = [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] |Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|府社|lt=Fu-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |Established = Traditionally, 39th year of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] |Main hall = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] (thatch roof) |Other names = Moto-Ise Kōtai Jingū, Moto-Ise Naikū |Festival date = {{ill|April 26|en|4月26日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 26|qq=}} |Rituals = Hassaku Parade ({{ill|September 1|en|9月1日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 1|qq=}}) }} '''Kōtai Shrine''' (皇大神社, '''Kōtai Jinja''') is a shrine located in the Ōe district of [[:en:Fukuchiyama, Kyoto|Fukuchiyama]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Moto-Ise|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} legend as one of the temporary enshrinements of [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] before settling in [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. == Overview == Amaterasu Ōmikami, now enshrined at [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]], was traditionally worshipped within the Imperial Palace. To relieve the awe of this presence, Emperor Sujin ordered the search for a suitable enshrinement location in the 6th year of his reign. After many relocations, the deity settled in Ise in the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign. The temporary enshrinement sites along this journey are remembered as the {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}}. Kōtai Shrine is one of these Moto-Ise sites, located at the historical location known as Yoshisa-no-Miya (吉佐宮). As such, it is also referred to as {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢内宮 皇大神社|Moto-Ise Shrines=Moto-Ise Shrines|lt=Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, reflecting its connection to the {{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of Ise Grand Shrine. Other Moto-Ise shrines in the Ōe area are: {|class="wikitable" |- !colspan="3" | {{ill|Three Moto-Ise Shrines of Fukuchiyama|ja|元伊勢三社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Moto-Ise Shrines of Fukuchiyama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- |'''{{ill|Kōtai Shrine|lt=Kōtai Shrine|lt=Kotai Shrine|ja|皇大神社 (福知山市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotai Shrine|qq=}}''' |Ōe-chō Naikū |Associated with the Inner Shrine (Naikū) of Ise Grand Shrine |- |'''{{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社|lt=Toyouke Daijinja|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|qq=}}''' |Ōe-chō Tadauchi |Associated with the Outer Shrine (Gekū) of Ise Grand Shrine |- |'''{{ill|Amanoiwato_Shrine_(Fukuchiyama)|ja|天岩戸神社_(福知山市)|lt=Amanoiwato Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanoiwato_Shrine_(Fukuchiyama)|qq=}}''' |Ōe-chō Busshōji |Kōtai Shrine’s auxiliary shrine |} Additional shrines also claim Yoshisa-no-Miya heritage, including {{ill|Kono Shrine|ja|籠神社#境外社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kono Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Sasahara Shrine|ja|笶原神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasahara Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Takeno Shrine|ja|竹野神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeno Shrine|qq=}}. == deity == * '''[[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]''' - Ancestral goddess of the Imperial family and deity of Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine). == History == According to the ''{{ill|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=}}'', in the 39th year of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}} brought Amaterasu Ōmikami to Yoshisa-no-Miya in Tanba Province for four years. Kōtai Shrine is believed to stand on this site, commemorating this temporary enshrinement. The shrine's records state that it was later reconstructed during the reign of [[:en:Empress Genmei|Empress Genmei]] (707–715). Alternative traditions link the shrine to {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Maroko|qq=}}, who reportedly established it during his suppression of local rebels. By the Edo period, Kōtai Shrine had significant support, including land grants and periodic reconstructions by the lords of Miyazu Domain. == Grounds == File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 本殿.JPG|'''Main Hall'''
Shinmei-zukuri with thatched roof. Features inward-curving [[:en:Chigi (architecture)|Chigi (architecture)]] and {{ill|Katsuogi|ja|鰹木|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuogi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 千木・鰹木}}. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 拝殿.JPG|'''Worship Hall''' File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 入口.JPG|'''Torii'''
A blackwood torii, similar to those at {{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|qq=}} and [[:en:Nonomiya Shrine|Nonomiya Shrine]]. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 和泉式部歌塚.JPG|'''Izumi Shikibu Monument''' The main hall is surrounded by 83 auxiliary shrines arranged in a U-shape. * Sacred Cedar of Ryūtō * Cedar planted by {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Maroko|qq=}} == Subsidiary Shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === Located to the left and right of the main hall: * Takuha Chichihime Shrine - Dedicated to [[:en:Takuhadachiji-hime|Takuhadachiji-hime]] * Amano-Tajikarao Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Amano-Tajikarao|ja|天手力雄命|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amano-Tajikarao|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tajikarao|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノタヂカラオ}} === Other Shrines === * 83 shrines surrounding the main hall. == Festivals == * '''Hassaku Festival''' (September 1): Includes a procession inspired by the feudal lord’s pilgrimage, reenacted with a daimyo-style parade. == Access == ; Address * Ōe-chō Naikū, Fukuchiyama City, Kyoto Prefecture 217 ; Transportation * '''By Train:''' 15-minute walk from [[:en:Ōeyamaguchi-Naiku Station|Ōeyamaguchi-Naikū Station]] on the {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Tango Railway|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to WILLER TRAINS}}. * '''By Car:''' Accessible via Kyoto Prefecture Route 9. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{commons cat|Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)}} * [http://motoise-naiku.com/ Official Website of Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine] * [https://www.uminokyoto.jp/genryu/fukuchiyama/page4.html Moto-Ise Shrines - Fukuchiyama City - Sea of Kyoto] (Kyoto Northern Region Promotion Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|皇大神社 (福知山市)|version=98851382|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Miyazu Domain]] }} [[sn:Kōtai Shrine]] [[ja:皇大神社 (福知山市)]] [[ja:皇大神社]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:Miyazu Domain]] [[Category:Buildings in Fukuchiyama, Kyoto]] {{Commons category|Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)}}

It is part of the {{ill|Moto-Ise|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} legend as one of the temporary enshrinements of [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] before settling in [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. == Overview == Amaterasu Ōmikami, now enshrined at [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]], was traditionally worshipped within the Imperial Palace. The temporary enshrinement sites along this journey are remembered as the {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}}. Kōtai Shrine is one of these Moto-Ise sites, located at the historical location known as Yoshisa-no-Miya (吉佐宮). Other Moto-Ise shrines in the Ōe area are: {|class="wikitable" |- !colspan="3" | {{ill|Three Moto-Ise Shrines of Fukuchiyama|ja|元伊勢三社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Moto-Ise Shrines of Fukuchiyama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- |'''{{ill|Kōtai Shrine|lt=Kōtai Shrine|lt=Kotai Shrine|ja|皇大神社 (福知山市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotai Shrine|qq=}}''' |Ōe-chō Naikū |Associated with the Inner Shrine (Naikū) of Ise Grand Shrine |- |'''{{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社|lt=Toyouke Daijinja|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|qq=}}''' |Ōe-chō Tadauchi |Associated with the Outer Shrine (Gekū) of Ise Grand Shrine |- |'''{{ill|Amanoiwato_Shrine_(Fukuchiyama)|ja|天岩戸神社_(福知山市)|lt=Amanoiwato Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanoiwato_Shrine_(Fukuchiyama)|qq=}}''' |Ōe-chō Busshōji |Kōtai Shrine’s auxiliary shrine |} Additional shrines also claim Yoshisa-no-Miya heritage, including {{ill|Kono Shrine|ja|籠神社#境外社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kono Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Sasahara Shrine|ja|笶原神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasahara Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Takeno Shrine|ja|竹野神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeno Shrine|qq=}}. == deity == * '''[[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]''' - Ancestral goddess of the Imperial family and deity of Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine). == History == According to the ''{{ill|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=}}'', in the 39th year of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}} brought Amaterasu Ōmikami to Yoshisa-no-Miya in Tanba Province for four years. Kōtai Shrine is believed to stand on this site, commemorating this temporary enshrinement. The shrine's records state that it was later reconstructed during the reign of [[:en:Empress Genmei|Empress Genmei]] (707–715). Features inward-curving [[:en:Chigi (architecture)|Chigi (architecture)]] and {{ill|Katsuogi|ja|鰹木|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuogi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 千木・鰹木}}. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 拝殿.JPG|'''Worship Hall''' File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 入口.JPG|'''Torii'''
A blackwood torii, similar to those at {{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|qq=}} and [[:en:Nonomiya Shrine|Nonomiya Shrine]]. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 和泉式部歌塚.JPG|'''Izumi Shikibu Monument''' The main hall is surrounded by 83 auxiliary shrines arranged in a U-shape. * Sacred Cedar of Ryūtō * Cedar planted by {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Maroko|qq=}} == Subsidiary Shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === Located to the left and right of the main hall: * Takuha Chichihime Shrine - Dedicated to [[:en:Takuhadachiji-hime|Takuhadachiji-hime]] * Amano-Tajikarao Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Amano-Tajikarao|ja|天手力雄命|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amano-Tajikarao|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tajikarao|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノタヂカラオ}} === Other Shrines === * 83 shrines surrounding the main hall. == Festivals == * '''Hassaku Festival''' (September 1): Includes a procession inspired by the feudal lord’s pilgrimage, reenacted with a daimyo-style parade. == Access == ; Address * Ōe-chō Naikū, Fukuchiyama City, Kyoto Prefecture 217 ; Transportation * '''By Train:''' 15-minute walk from [[:en:Ōeyamaguchi-Naiku Station|Ōeyamaguchi-Naikū Station]] on the {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Tango Railway|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to WILLER TRAINS}}. * '''By Car:''' Accessible via Kyoto Prefecture Route 9. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{commons cat|Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)}} * [http://motoise-naiku.com/ Official Website of Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine] * [https://www.uminokyoto.jp/genryu/fukuchiyama/page4.html Moto-Ise Shrines - Fukuchiyama City - Sea of Kyoto] (Kyoto Northern Region Promotion Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|皇大神社 (福知山市)|version=98851382|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Miyazu Domain]] }} [[sn:Kōtai Shrine]] [[ja:皇大神社 (福知山市)]] [[ja:皇大神社]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:Miyazu Domain]] [[Category:Buildings in Fukuchiyama, Kyoto]] {{Commons category|Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)}}

The temporary enshrinement sites along this journey are remembered as the {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}}. Kōtai Shrine is one of these Moto-Ise sites, located at the historical location known as Yoshisa-no-Miya (吉佐宮). As such, it is also referred to as {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢内宮 皇大神社|Moto-Ise Shrines=Moto-Ise Shrines|lt=Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, reflecting its connection to the {{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of Ise Grand Shrine. Other Moto-Ise shrines in the Ōe area are: {|class="wikitable" |- !colspan="3" | {{ill|Three Moto-Ise Shrines of Fukuchiyama|ja|元伊勢三社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Moto-Ise Shrines of Fukuchiyama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- |'''{{ill|Kōtai Shrine|lt=Kōtai Shrine|lt=Kotai Shrine|ja|皇大神社 (福知山市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotai Shrine|qq=}}''' |Ōe-chō Naikū |Associated with the Inner Shrine (Naikū) of Ise Grand Shrine |- |'''{{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社|lt=Toyouke Daijinja|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|qq=}}''' |Ōe-chō Tadauchi |Associated with the Outer Shrine (Gekū) of Ise Grand Shrine |- |'''{{ill|Amanoiwato_Shrine_(Fukuchiyama)|ja|天岩戸神社_(福知山市)|lt=Amanoiwato Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanoiwato_Shrine_(Fukuchiyama)|qq=}}''' |Ōe-chō Busshōji |Kōtai Shrine’s auxiliary shrine |} Additional shrines also claim Yoshisa-no-Miya heritage, including {{ill|Kono Shrine|ja|籠神社#境外社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kono Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Sasahara Shrine|ja|笶原神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasahara Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Takeno Shrine|ja|竹野神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeno Shrine|qq=}}. == deity == * '''[[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]''' - Ancestral goddess of the Imperial family and deity of Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine). == History == According to the ''{{ill|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=}}'', in the 39th year of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}} brought Amaterasu Ōmikami to Yoshisa-no-Miya in Tanba Province for four years. Kōtai Shrine is believed to stand on this site, commemorating this temporary enshrinement. The shrine's records state that it was later reconstructed during the reign of [[:en:Empress Genmei|Empress Genmei]] (707–715). Alternative traditions link the shrine to {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Maroko|qq=}}, who reportedly established it during his suppression of local rebels. By the Edo period, Kōtai Shrine had significant support, including land grants and periodic reconstructions by the lords of Miyazu Domain. == Grounds == File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 本殿.JPG|'''Main Hall'''
Shinmei-zukuri with thatched roof. Features inward-curving [[:en:Chigi (architecture)|Chigi (architecture)]] and {{ill|Katsuogi|ja|鰹木|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuogi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 千木・鰹木}}. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 拝殿.JPG|'''Worship Hall''' File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 入口.JPG|'''Torii'''
A blackwood torii, similar to those at {{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|qq=}} and [[:en:Nonomiya Shrine|Nonomiya Shrine]]. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 和泉式部歌塚.JPG|'''Izumi Shikibu Monument''' The main hall is surrounded by 83 auxiliary shrines arranged in a U-shape. * Sacred Cedar of Ryūtō * Cedar planted by {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Maroko|qq=}} == Subsidiary Shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === Located to the left and right of the main hall: * Takuha Chichihime Shrine - Dedicated to [[:en:Takuhadachiji-hime|Takuhadachiji-hime]] * Amano-Tajikarao Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Amano-Tajikarao|ja|天手力雄命|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amano-Tajikarao|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tajikarao|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノタヂカラオ}} === Other Shrines === * 83 shrines surrounding the main hall. == Festivals == * '''Hassaku Festival''' (September 1): Includes a procession inspired by the feudal lord’s pilgrimage, reenacted with a daimyo-style parade. == Access == ; Address * Ōe-chō Naikū, Fukuchiyama City, Kyoto Prefecture 217 ; Transportation * '''By Train:''' 15-minute walk from [[:en:Ōeyamaguchi-Naiku Station|Ōeyamaguchi-Naikū Station]] on the {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Tango Railway|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to WILLER TRAINS}}. * '''By Car:''' Accessible via Kyoto Prefecture Route 9. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{commons cat|Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)}} * [http://motoise-naiku.com/ Official Website of Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine] * [https://www.uminokyoto.jp/genryu/fukuchiyama/page4.html Moto-Ise Shrines - Fukuchiyama City - Sea of Kyoto] (Kyoto Northern Region Promotion Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|皇大神社 (福知山市)|version=98851382|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Miyazu Domain]] }} [[sn:Kōtai Shrine]] [[ja:皇大神社 (福知山市)]] [[ja:皇大神社]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:Miyazu Domain]] [[Category:Buildings in Fukuchiyama, Kyoto]] {{Commons category|Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11581010]] Wikidata name: Kōtaijinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{otheruses}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kōtai Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kotaijinzya shrine.jpg |location = [[:en:Fukuchiyama, Kyoto|Fukuchiyama]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]] Ōe-chō Naikū Aza Miyama 217 |coordinates = {{Coord|35.430139|N|135.1545|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602}} |Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|府社|lt=Fu-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |Established = Traditionally, 39th year of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] |Main hall = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] (thatch roof) |Other names = Moto-Ise Kōtai Jingū, Moto-Ise Naikū |Festival date = {{ill|April 26|en|4月26日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 26|qq=}} |Rituals = Hassaku Parade ({{ill|September 1|en|9月1日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 1|qq=}}) |prefecture= {{ill|Fukuchiyama|qid=Q3355306}} |architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545}} }} '''Kōtai Shrine''' (皇大神社, '''Kōtai Jinja''') is a shrine located in the Ōe district of [[:en:Fukuchiyama, Kyoto|Fukuchiyama]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Moto-Ise|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} legend as one of the temporary enshrinements of [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] before settling in [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. == Overview == Amaterasu Ōmikami, now enshrined at [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]], was traditionally worshipped within the Imperial Palace. To relieve the awe of this presence, Emperor Sujin ordered the search for a suitable enshrinement location in the 6th year of his reign. After many relocations, the deity settled in Ise in the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign. The temporary enshrinement sites along this journey are remembered as the {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}}. Kōtai Shrine is one of these Moto-Ise sites, located at the historical location known as Yoshisa-no-Miya (吉佐宮). As such, it is also referred to as {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢内宮 皇大神社|Moto-Ise Shrines=Moto-Ise Shrines|lt=Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, reflecting its connection to the {{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of Ise Grand Shrine. Kōtai Shrine is believed to stand on this site, commemorating this temporary enshrinement. The shrine's records state that it was later reconstructed during the reign of [[:en:Empress Genmei|Empress Genmei]] (707–715). Alternative traditions link the shrine to {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Maroko|qq=}}, who reportedly established it during his suppression of local rebels. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 和泉式部歌塚.JPG|'''Izumi Shikibu Monument''' The main hall is surrounded by 83 auxiliary shrines arranged in a U-shape. * Sacred Cedar of Ryūtō * Cedar planted by {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Maroko|qq=}} == Subsidiary Shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === Located to the left and right of the main hall: * Takuha Chichihime Shrine - Dedicated to [[:en:Takuhadachiji-hime|Takuhadachiji-hime]] * Amano-Tajikarao Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Amano-Tajikarao|ja|天手力雄命|1=Ame-no-tajikarao|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amano-Tajikarao|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tajikarao|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノタヂカラオ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q849412}} === Other Shrines === * 83 shrines surrounding the main hall. == Festivals == * '''Hassaku Festival''' (September 1): Includes a procession inspired by the feudal lord’s pilgrimage, reenacted with a daimyo-style parade. == Access == ; Address * Ōe-chō Naikū, Fukuchiyama City, Kyoto Prefecture 217 ; Transportation * '''By Train:''' 15-minute walk from [[:en:Ōeyamaguchi-Naiku Station|Ōeyamaguchi-Naikū Station]] on the {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Tango Railway|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to WILLER TRAINS|1=Willer Trains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19727758}}. * '''By Car:''' Accessible via Kyoto Prefecture Route 9. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{commons cat|Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)}} * [http://motoise-naiku.com/ Official Website of Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine] * [https://www.uminokyoto.jp/genryu/fukuchiyama/page4.html Moto-Ise Shrines - Fukuchiyama City - Sea of Kyoto] (Kyoto Northern Region Promotion Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|皇大神社 (福知山市)|version=98851382|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Miyazu Domain]] }} [[sn:Kōtai Shrine]] [[ja:皇大神社 (福知山市)]] [[ja:皇大神社]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:Miyazu Domain]] [[Category:Buildings in Fukuchiyama, Kyoto]] {{Commons category|Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11581010]] Wikidata name: Kōtaijinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{otheruses}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kōtai Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kotaijinzya shrine.jpg |location = [[:en:Fukuchiyama, Kyoto|Fukuchiyama]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]] Ōe-chō Naikū Aza Miyama 217 |coordinates = {{Coord|35.430139|N|135.1545|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602}} |Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|府社|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Established = Traditionally, 39th year of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] |Main hall = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] (thatch roof) |Other names = Moto-Ise Kōtai Jingū, Moto-Ise Naikū |Festival date = {{ill|April 26|en|4月26日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 26|qq=}} |Rituals = Hassaku Parade ({{ill|September 1|en|9月1日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 1|qq=}}) |prefecture= {{ill|Fukuchiyama|qid=Q3355306}} |architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545}} }} '''Kōtai Shrine''' (皇大神社, '''Kōtai Jinja''') is a shrine located in the Ōe district of [[:en:Fukuchiyama, Kyoto|Fukuchiyama]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Moto-Ise|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} legend as one of the temporary enshrinements of [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] before settling in [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. == Overview == Amaterasu Ōmikami, now enshrined at [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]], was traditionally worshipped within the Imperial Palace. To relieve the awe of this presence, Emperor Sujin ordered the search for a suitable enshrinement location in the 6th year of his reign. After many relocations, the deity settled in Ise in the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign. The temporary enshrinement sites along this journey are remembered as the {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}}. Kōtai Shrine is one of these Moto-Ise sites, located at the historical location known as Yoshisa-no-Miya (吉佐宮). As such, it is also referred to as {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢内宮 皇大神社|Moto-Ise Shrines=Moto-Ise Shrines|lt=Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, reflecting its connection to the {{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of Ise Grand Shrine. Other Moto-Ise shrines in the Ōe area are: {|class="wikitable" |- !colspan="3" | {{ill|Three Moto-Ise Shrines of Fukuchiyama|ja|元伊勢三社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Moto-Ise Shrines of Fukuchiyama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- |'''{{ill|Kōtai Shrine|lt=Kōtai Shrine|lt=Kotai Shrine|ja|皇大神社 (福知山市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotai Shrine|qq=}}''' |Ōe-chō Naikū |Associated with the Inner Shrine (Naikū) of Ise Grand Shrine |- |'''{{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社|lt=Toyouke Daijinja|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|qq=}}''' |Ōe-chō Tadauchi |Associated with the Outer Shrine (Gekū) of Ise Grand Shrine |- |'''{{ill|Amanoiwato_Shrine_(Fukuchiyama)|ja|天岩戸神社_(福知山市)|lt=Amanoiwato Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanoiwato_Shrine_(Fukuchiyama)|qq=}}''' |Ōe-chō Busshōji |Kōtai Shrine’s auxiliary shrine |} Additional shrines also claim Yoshisa-no-Miya heritage, including {{ill|Kono Shrine|ja|籠神社#境外社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kono Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Sasahara Shrine|ja|笶原神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasahara Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Takeno Shrine|ja|竹野神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeno Shrine|qq=}}. == deity == * '''[[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]''' - Ancestral goddess of the Imperial family and deity of Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine). == History == According to the ''{{ill|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=}}'', in the 39th year of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}} brought Amaterasu Ōmikami to Yoshisa-no-Miya in Tanba Province for four years. Kōtai Shrine is believed to stand on this site, commemorating this temporary enshrinement. The shrine's records state that it was later reconstructed during the reign of [[:en:Empress Genmei|Empress Genmei]] (707–715). Alternative traditions link the shrine to {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Maroko|qq=}}, who reportedly established it during his suppression of local rebels. By the Edo period, Kōtai Shrine had significant support, including land grants and periodic reconstructions by the lords of Miyazu Domain. == Grounds == File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 本殿.JPG|'''Main Hall'''
Shinmei-zukuri with thatched roof. Features inward-curving [[:en:Chigi (architecture)|Chigi (architecture)]] and {{ill|Katsuogi|ja|鰹木|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuogi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 千木・鰹木}}. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 拝殿.JPG|'''Worship Hall''' File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 入口.JPG|'''Torii'''
A blackwood torii, similar to those at {{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|qq=}} and [[:en:Nonomiya Shrine|Nonomiya Shrine]]. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 和泉式部歌塚.JPG|'''Izumi Shikibu Monument''' The main hall is surrounded by 83 auxiliary shrines arranged in a U-shape. * Sacred Cedar of Ryūtō * Cedar planted by {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Maroko|qq=}} == Subsidiary Shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === Located to the left and right of the main hall: * Takuha Chichihime Shrine - Dedicated to [[:en:Takuhadachiji-hime|Takuhadachiji-hime]] * Amano-Tajikarao Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Amano-Tajikarao|ja|天手力雄命|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amano-Tajikarao|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tajikarao|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノタヂカラオ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q849412}} === Other Shrines === * 83 shrines surrounding the main hall. == Festivals == * '''Hassaku Festival''' (September 1): Includes a procession inspired by the feudal lord’s pilgrimage, reenacted with a daimyo-style parade. == Access == ; Address * Ōe-chō Naikū, Fukuchiyama City, Kyoto Prefecture 217 ; Transportation * '''By Train:''' 15-minute walk from [[:en:Ōeyamaguchi-Naiku Station|Ōeyamaguchi-Naikū Station]] on the {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Tango Railway|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to WILLER TRAINS|1=Willer Trains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19727758}}. * '''By Car:''' Accessible via Kyoto Prefecture Route 9. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{commons cat|Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)}} * [http://motoise-naiku.com/ Official Website of Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine] * [https://www.uminokyoto.jp/genryu/fukuchiyama/page4.html Moto-Ise Shrines - Fukuchiyama City - Sea of Kyoto] (Kyoto Northern Region Promotion Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|皇大神社 (福知山市)|version=98851382|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Miyazu Domain]] }} [[sn:Kōtai Shrine]] [[ja:皇大神社 (福知山市)]] [[ja:皇大神社]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:Miyazu Domain]] [[Category:Buildings in Fukuchiyama, Kyoto]] {{Commons category|Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11581010]] Wikidata name: Kōtaijinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{otheruses}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kōtai Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kotaijinzya shrine.jpg |location = [[:en:Fukuchiyama, Kyoto|Fukuchiyama]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]] Ōe-chō Naikū Aza Miyama 217 |coordinates = {{Coord|35.430139|N|135.1545|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602}} |Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|qid=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Established = Traditionally, 39th year of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] |Main hall = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] (thatch roof) |Other names = Moto-Ise Kōtai Jingū, Moto-Ise Naikū |Festival date = {{ill|April 26|en|4月26日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 26|qq=}} |Rituals = Hassaku Parade ({{ill|September 1|en|9月1日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 1|qq=}}) |prefecture= {{ill|Fukuchiyama|qid=Q3355306}} |architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545}} }} '''Kōtai Shrine''' (皇大神社, '''Kōtai Jinja''') is a shrine located in the Ōe district of [[:en:Fukuchiyama, Kyoto|Fukuchiyama]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Moto-Ise|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} legend as one of the temporary enshrinements of [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] before settling in [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. == Overview == Amaterasu Ōmikami, now enshrined at [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]], was traditionally worshipped within the Imperial Palace. To relieve the awe of this presence, Emperor Sujin ordered the search for a suitable enshrinement location in the 6th year of his reign. After many relocations, the deity settled in Ise in the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign. The temporary enshrinement sites along this journey are remembered as the {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}}. Kōtai Shrine is one of these Moto-Ise sites, located at the historical location known as Yoshisa-no-Miya (吉佐宮). As such, it is also referred to as {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢内宮 皇大神社|Moto-Ise Shrines=Moto-Ise Shrines|lt=Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, reflecting its connection to the {{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of Ise Grand Shrine. Other Moto-Ise shrines in the Ōe area are: {|class="wikitable" |- !colspan="3" | {{ill|Three Moto-Ise Shrines of Fukuchiyama|ja|元伊勢三社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Moto-Ise Shrines of Fukuchiyama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- |'''{{ill|Kōtai Shrine|lt=Kōtai Shrine|lt=Kotai Shrine|ja|皇大神社 (福知山市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotai Shrine|qq=}}''' |Ōe-chō Naikū |Associated with the Inner Shrine (Naikū) of Ise Grand Shrine |- |'''{{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社|lt=Toyouke Daijinja|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|qq=}}''' |Ōe-chō Tadauchi |Associated with the Outer Shrine (Gekū) of Ise Grand Shrine |- |'''{{ill|Amanoiwato_Shrine_(Fukuchiyama)|ja|天岩戸神社_(福知山市)|lt=Amanoiwato Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanoiwato_Shrine_(Fukuchiyama)|qq=}}''' |Ōe-chō Busshōji |Kōtai Shrine’s auxiliary shrine |} Additional shrines also claim Yoshisa-no-Miya heritage, including {{ill|Kono Shrine|ja|籠神社#境外社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kono Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Sasahara Shrine|ja|笶原神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasahara Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Takeno Shrine|ja|竹野神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeno Shrine|qq=}}. == deity == * '''[[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]''' - Ancestral goddess of the Imperial family and deity of Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine). == History == According to the ''{{ill|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=}}'', in the 39th year of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}} brought Amaterasu Ōmikami to Yoshisa-no-Miya in Tanba Province for four years. Kōtai Shrine is believed to stand on this site, commemorating this temporary enshrinement. The shrine's records state that it was later reconstructed during the reign of [[:en:Empress Genmei|Empress Genmei]] (707–715). Alternative traditions link the shrine to {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Maroko|qq=}}, who reportedly established it during his suppression of local rebels. By the Edo period, Kōtai Shrine had significant support, including land grants and periodic reconstructions by the lords of Miyazu Domain. == Grounds == File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 本殿.JPG|'''Main Hall'''
Shinmei-zukuri with thatched roof. Features inward-curving [[:en:Chigi (architecture)|Chigi (architecture)]] and {{ill|Katsuogi|ja|鰹木|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuogi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 千木・鰹木}}. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 拝殿.JPG|'''Worship Hall''' File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 入口.JPG|'''Torii'''
A blackwood torii, similar to those at {{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|qq=}} and [[:en:Nonomiya Shrine|Nonomiya Shrine]]. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 和泉式部歌塚.JPG|'''Izumi Shikibu Monument''' The main hall is surrounded by 83 auxiliary shrines arranged in a U-shape. * Sacred Cedar of Ryūtō * Cedar planted by {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Maroko|qq=}} == Subsidiary Shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === Located to the left and right of the main hall: * Takuha Chichihime Shrine - Dedicated to [[:en:Takuhadachiji-hime|Takuhadachiji-hime]] * Amano-Tajikarao Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Amano-Tajikarao|ja|天手力雄命|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amano-Tajikarao|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tajikarao|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノタヂカラオ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q849412}} === Other Shrines === * 83 shrines surrounding the main hall. == Festivals == * '''Hassaku Festival''' (September 1): Includes a procession inspired by the feudal lord’s pilgrimage, reenacted with a daimyo-style parade. == Access == ; Address * Ōe-chō Naikū, Fukuchiyama City, Kyoto Prefecture 217 ; Transportation * '''By Train:''' 15-minute walk from [[:en:Ōeyamaguchi-Naiku Station|Ōeyamaguchi-Naikū Station]] on the {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Tango Railway|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to WILLER TRAINS|1=Willer Trains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19727758}}. * '''By Car:''' Accessible via Kyoto Prefecture Route 9. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{commons cat|Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)}} * [http://motoise-naiku.com/ Official Website of Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine] * [https://www.uminokyoto.jp/genryu/fukuchiyama/page4.html Moto-Ise Shrines - Fukuchiyama City - Sea of Kyoto] (Kyoto Northern Region Promotion Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|皇大神社 (福知山市)|version=98851382|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Miyazu Domain]] }} [[sn:Kōtai Shrine]] [[ja:皇大神社 (福知山市)]] [[ja:皇大神社]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:Miyazu Domain]] [[Category:Buildings in Fukuchiyama, Kyoto]] {{Commons category|Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11581010]] Wikidata name: Kōtaijinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{otheruses}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kōtai Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kotaijinzya shrine.jpg |location = [[:en:Fukuchiyama, Kyoto|Fukuchiyama]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]] Ōe-chō Naikū Aza Miyama 217 |coordinates = {{Coord|35.430139|N|135.1545|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602}} |Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|qid=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Established = Traditionally, 39th year of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] |Main hall = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] (thatch roof) |Other names = Moto-Ise Kōtai Jingū, Moto-Ise Naikū |Festival date = {{ill|April 26|en|4月26日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 26|qq=}} |Rituals = Hassaku Parade ({{ill|September 1|en|9月1日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 1|qq=}}) |prefecture= {{ill|Fukuchiyama|qid=Q3355306}} |architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545}} }} '''Kōtai Shrine''' (皇大神社, '''Kōtai Jinja''') is a shrine located in the Ōe district of [[:en:Fukuchiyama, Kyoto|Fukuchiyama]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Moto-Ise|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} legend as one of the temporary enshrinements of [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] before settling in [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. == Overview == Amaterasu Ōmikami, now enshrined at [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]], was traditionally worshipped within the Imperial Palace. To relieve the awe of this presence, Emperor Sujin ordered the search for a suitable enshrinement location in the 6th year of his reign. After many relocations, the deity settled in Ise in the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign. The temporary enshrinement sites along this journey are remembered as the {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}}. Kōtai Shrine is one of these Moto-Ise sites, located at the historical location known as Yoshisa-no-Miya (吉佐宮). As such, it is also referred to as {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢内宮 皇大神社|Moto-Ise Shrines=Moto-Ise Shrines|lt=Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, reflecting its connection to the {{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of Ise Grand Shrine. Other Moto-Ise shrines in the Ōe area are: {|class="wikitable" |- !colspan="3" | {{ill|Three Moto-Ise Shrines of Fukuchiyama|ja|元伊勢三社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Moto-Ise Shrines of Fukuchiyama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- |'''{{ill|Kōtai Shrine|lt=Kōtai Shrine|lt=Kotai Shrine|ja|皇大神社 (福知山市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotai Shrine|qq=}}''' |Ōe-chō Naikū |Associated with the Inner Shrine (Naikū) of Ise Grand Shrine |- |'''{{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社|lt=Toyouke Daijinja|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|qq=}}''' |Ōe-chō Tadauchi |Associated with the Outer Shrine (Gekū) of Ise Grand Shrine |- |'''{{ill|Amanoiwato_Shrine_(Fukuchiyama)|ja|天岩戸神社_(福知山市)|lt=Amanoiwato Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanoiwato_Shrine_(Fukuchiyama)|qq=}}''' |Ōe-chō Busshōji |Kōtai Shrine’s auxiliary shrine |} Additional shrines also claim Yoshisa-no-Miya heritage, including {{ill|Kono Shrine|ja|籠神社#境外社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kono Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Sasahara Shrine|ja|笶原神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasahara Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Takeno Shrine|ja|竹野神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeno Shrine|qq=}}. == deity == * '''[[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]''' - Ancestral goddess of the Imperial family and deity of Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine). == History == According to the ''{{ill|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=}}'', in the 39th year of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}} brought Amaterasu Ōmikami to Yoshisa-no-Miya in Tanba Province for four years. Kōtai Shrine is believed to stand on this site, commemorating this temporary enshrinement. The shrine's records state that it was later reconstructed during the reign of [[:en:Empress Genmei|Empress Genmei]] (707–715). Alternative traditions link the shrine to {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Maroko|qq=}}, who reportedly established it during his suppression of local rebels. By the Edo period, Kōtai Shrine had significant support, including land grants and periodic reconstructions by the lords of Miyazu Domain. == Grounds == File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 本殿.JPG|'''Main Hall'''
Shinmei-zukuri with thatched roof. Features inward-curving [[:en:Chigi (architecture)|Chigi (architecture)]] and {{ill|Katsuogi|ja|鰹木|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuogi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 千木・鰹木}}. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 拝殿.JPG|'''Worship Hall''' File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 入口.JPG|'''Torii'''
A blackwood torii, similar to those at {{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|qq=}} and [[:en:Nonomiya Shrine|Nonomiya Shrine]]. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 和泉式部歌塚.JPG|'''Izumi Shikibu Monument''' The main hall is surrounded by 83 auxiliary shrines arranged in a U-shape. * Sacred Cedar of Ryūtō * Cedar planted by {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Maroko|qq=}} == Subsidiary Shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === Located to the left and right of the main hall: * Takuha Chichihime Shrine - Dedicated to [[:en:Takuhadachiji-hime|Takuhadachiji-hime]] * Amano-Tajikarao Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Amano-Tajikarao|ja|天手力雄命|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amano-Tajikarao|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tajikarao|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノタヂカラオ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q849412}} === Other Shrines === * 83 shrines surrounding the main hall. == Festivals == * '''Hassaku Festival''' (September 1): Includes a procession inspired by the feudal lord’s pilgrimage, reenacted with a daimyo-style parade. == Access == ; Address * Ōe-chō Naikū, Fukuchiyama City, Kyoto Prefecture 217 ; Transportation * '''By Train:''' 15-minute walk from [[:en:Ōeyamaguchi-Naiku Station|Ōeyamaguchi-Naikū Station]] on the {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Tango Railway|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to WILLER TRAINS|1=Willer Trains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19727758}}. * '''By Car:''' Accessible via Kyoto Prefecture Route 9. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{commons cat|Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)}} * [http://motoise-naiku.com/ Official Website of Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine] * [https://www.uminokyoto.jp/genryu/fukuchiyama/page4.html Moto-Ise Shrines - Fukuchiyama City - Sea of Kyoto] (Kyoto Northern Region Promotion Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|皇大神社 (福知山市)|version=98851382|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Miyazu Domain]] }} [[sn:Kōtai Shrine]] [[ja:皇大神社 (福知山市)]] [[ja:皇大神社]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:Miyazu Domain]] [[Category:Buildings in Fukuchiyama, Kyoto]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kōtai Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{Commons category|Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)}}

To relieve the awe of this presence, Emperor Sujin ordered the search for a suitable enshrinement location in the 6th year of his reign. After many relocations, the deity settled in Ise in the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign. The temporary enshrinement sites along this journey are remembered as the {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}}. Address * Ōe-chō Naikū, Fukuchiyama City, Kyoto Prefecture 217 ; Transportation * '''By Train:''' 15-minute walk from [[:en:Ōeyamaguchi-Naiku Station|Ōeyamaguchi-Naikū Station]] on the {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Tango Railway|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to WILLER TRAINS|1=Willer Trains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19727758}}. * '''By Car:''' Accessible via Kyoto Prefecture Route 9. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{commons cat|Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)}} * [http://motoise-naiku.com/ Official Website of Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine] * [https://www.uminokyoto.jp/genryu/fukuchiyama/page4.html Moto-Ise Shrines - Fukuchiyama City - Sea of Kyoto] (Kyoto Northern Region Promotion Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|皇大神社 (福知山市)|version=98851382|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Miyazu Domain]] }} [[sn:Kōtai Shrine]] [[ja:皇大神社 (福知山市)]] [[ja:皇大神社]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:Miyazu Domain]] [[Category:Buildings in Fukuchiyama, Kyoto]] [[sn:Kōtai Shrine]] {{Commons category|Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11581010]] Wikidata name: Kōtaijinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{otheruses}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kōtai Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kotaijinzya shrine.jpg |location = [[:en:Fukuchiyama, Kyoto|Fukuchiyama]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]] Ōe-chō Naikū Aza Miyama 217 |coordinates = {{Coord|35.430139|N|135.1545|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602}} |Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|qid=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Established = Traditionally, 39th year of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] |Main hall = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] (thatch roof) |Other names = Moto-Ise Kōtai Jingū, Moto-Ise Naikū |Festival date = {{ill|April 26|en|4月26日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 26|qq=}} |Rituals = Hassaku Parade ({{ill|September 1|en|9月1日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 1|qq=}}) |prefecture= {{ill|Fukuchiyama|qid=Q3355306}} |architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545}} }} '''Kōtai Shrine''' (皇大神社, '''Kōtai Jinja''') is a shrine located in the Ōe district of [[:en:Fukuchiyama, Kyoto|Fukuchiyama]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Moto-Ise|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} legend as one of the temporary enshrinements of [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] before settling in [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. == Overview == Amaterasu Ōmikami, now enshrined at [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]], was traditionally worshipped within the Imperial Palace. To relieve the awe of this presence, Emperor Sujin ordered the search for a suitable enshrinement location in the 6th year of his reign. After many relocations, the deity settled in Ise in the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign. The temporary enshrinement sites along this journey are remembered as the {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}}. Kōtai Shrine is one of these Moto-Ise sites, located at the historical location known as Yoshisa-no-Miya (吉佐宮). As such, it is also referred to as {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢内宮 皇大神社|Moto-Ise Shrines=Moto-Ise Shrines|lt=Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, reflecting its connection to the {{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of Ise Grand Shrine. Other Moto-Ise shrines in the Ōe area are: {|class="wikitable" |- !colspan="3" | {{ill|Three Moto-Ise Shrines of Fukuchiyama|ja|元伊勢三社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Moto-Ise Shrines of Fukuchiyama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- |'''{{ill|Kōtai Shrine|lt=Kōtai Shrine|lt=Kotai Shrine|ja|皇大神社 (福知山市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotai Shrine|qq=}}''' |Ōe-chō Naikū |Associated with the Inner Shrine (Naikū) of Ise Grand Shrine |- |'''{{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社|lt=Toyouke Daijinja|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|qq=}}''' |Ōe-chō Tadauchi |Associated with the Outer Shrine (Gekū) of Ise Grand Shrine |- |'''{{ill|Amanoiwato_Shrine_(Fukuchiyama)|ja|天岩戸神社_(福知山市)|lt=Amanoiwato Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanoiwato_Shrine_(Fukuchiyama)|qq=}}''' |Ōe-chō Busshōji |Kōtai Shrine’s auxiliary shrine |} Additional shrines also claim Yoshisa-no-Miya heritage, including {{ill|Kono Shrine|ja|籠神社#境外社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kono Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Sasahara Shrine|ja|笶原神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasahara Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Takeno Shrine|ja|竹野神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeno Shrine|qq=}}. == deity == * '''[[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]''' - Ancestral goddess of the Imperial family and deity of Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine). == History == According to the ''{{ill|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=}}'', in the 39th year of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}} brought Amaterasu Ōmikami to Yoshisa-no-Miya in Tanba Province for four years. Kōtai Shrine is believed to stand on this site, commemorating this temporary enshrinement. The shrine's records state that it was later reconstructed during the reign of [[:en:Empress Genmei|Empress Genmei]] (707–715). Alternative traditions link the shrine to {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Maroko|qq=}}, who reportedly established it during his suppression of local rebels. By the Edo period, Kōtai Shrine had significant support, including land grants and periodic reconstructions by the lords of Miyazu Domain. == Grounds == File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 本殿.JPG|'''Main Hall'''
Shinmei-zukuri with thatched roof. Features inward-curving [[:en:Chigi (architecture)|Chigi (architecture)]] and {{ill|Katsuogi|ja|鰹木|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuogi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 千木・鰹木}}. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 拝殿.JPG|'''Worship Hall''' File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 入口.JPG|'''Torii'''
A blackwood torii, similar to those at {{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|qq=}} and [[:en:Nonomiya Shrine|Nonomiya Shrine]]. File:皇大神社 (福知山市) 和泉式部歌塚.JPG|'''Izumi Shikibu Monument''' The main hall is surrounded by 83 auxiliary shrines arranged in a U-shape. * Sacred Cedar of Ryūtō * Cedar planted by {{ill|Prince Maroko|ja|当麻皇子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Maroko|qq=}} == Subsidiary Shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === Located to the left and right of the main hall: * Takuha Chichihime Shrine - Dedicated to [[:en:Takuhadachiji-hime|Takuhadachiji-hime]] * Amano-Tajikarao Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Amano-Tajikarao|ja|天手力雄命|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amano-Tajikarao|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tajikarao|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノタヂカラオ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q849412}} === Other Shrines === * 83 shrines surrounding the main hall. == Festivals == * '''Hassaku Festival''' (September 1): Includes a procession inspired by the feudal lord’s pilgrimage, reenacted with a daimyo-style parade. == Access == ; Address * Ōe-chō Naikū, Fukuchiyama City, Kyoto Prefecture 217 ; Transportation * '''By Train:''' 15-minute walk from [[:en:Ōeyamaguchi-Naiku Station|Ōeyamaguchi-Naikū Station]] on the {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Tango Railway|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to WILLER TRAINS|1=Willer Trains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19727758}}. * '''By Car:''' Accessible via Kyoto Prefecture Route 9. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{commons cat|Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)}} * [http://motoise-naiku.com/ Official Website of Moto-Ise Naikū Kōtai Shrine] * [https://www.uminokyoto.jp/genryu/fukuchiyama/page4.html Moto-Ise Shrines - Fukuchiyama City - Sea of Kyoto] (Kyoto Northern Region Promotion Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|皇大神社 (福知山市)|version=98851382|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Miyazu Domain]] }} [[sn:Kōtai Shrine]] [[ja:皇大神社 (福知山市)]] [[ja:皇大神社]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:Miyazu Domain]] [[Category:Buildings in Fukuchiyama, Kyoto]] [[sn:Kōtai Shrine]] {{Commons category|Kōtaijinja (Fukuchiyama)}} {{wikidata link|Q11581010}}
